programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: St Michael's is used as the location for a Hollywood movie, but a series of life-threatening accidents casts a pall over proceedings. Crime drama, with Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: A gun-toting nun fixes Sister Steve squarely in her sights during the silver jubilee celebrations at a convent - and it is up to Father Dowling to save the day. Tom Bosley stars as the crime-busting cleric

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble becomes a cutman for a fighter who knows he has brain damage but refuses to quit

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A young pharmacist who wants to be a television writer stumbles over a dead body and is accused of murder.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker's ex-con friend goes undercover to bust open a gang of criminals. Adventure, starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio investigates the apparent suicide of an anaesthetist who worked in a cosmetic surgery clinic, only to discover foul play was involved. Investigations lead him to a bereaved widower who believes the victim was responsible for his wife's death

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: One of Tony's old murder investigations is reopened and, in an attempt to find the perpetrator, he decides to switch roles with Gibbs, leading the team in an inquiry that links the case to an embezzlement scheme. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Sasha Alexander, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble finds himself distrusted when he joins a group of fruit harvesters, and must win them over

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: An immature young woman is the prime suspect when her domineering legal guardian is found murdered.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The marine son of a colonel-turned-preacher is found dead in woodlands after changing his religious persuasion. As the case unfolds, it becomes apparent to the agents that he could have been the victim of a hate crime. Meanwhile, Gibbs' father visits for the holidays. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part two of two. Walker must summon the courage to tell seven-year-old Lucas he has contracted Aids from his mother. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when three dehydrated survivors from a helicopter crash are discovered. The pilot is initially missing, but his body later washes ashore with a gunshot wound to the head. Things get complicated when one of the crew members tells the agents the chopper was hit by another aircraft, while another states that the pilot tried to kill them all by crashing into the sea on purpose. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: On a live-fire air force training exercise, someone hacks into the control systems of a drone and fires one of its two live missiles, striking and killing a serviceman, before piloting it away. Sam and Callen meet with a member of the National Security Agency, while Eric's inquiries lead him to a group of students on an advanced computer science course and Kensi is sent under cover to pose as its guest speaker

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy pilot who was wearing an experimental jet pack is murdered in a secluded area, and the strange circumstances of the case lead the team into a confrontation with a no-nonsense attorney who appears to hold a grudge against Gibbs. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A man who stole a car and ran down and killed one of his pursuers before escaping is identified as Walton Flynn, an ex-marine now working for a private security firm in Iraq. Sam and Callen learn that the businessman he had been hired to protect had been murdered and that the people chasing him were hired by his employers to dish out their own form of justice. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is taken aback to discover Tony's father is linked to the team's latest investigation, a politically sensitive case involving an assassination attempt on Prince Sayif, a visiting Saudi dignitary with a taste for the high life. programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The emotional experience of delivering a baby together helps to reconcile Peggy and Ruth, while Vic learns the reason behind Stephen leaving university. Elsewhere, Mary Cooper causes chaos when she gets behind the wheel of a car. Sarah Lancashire, Pam Ferris, Tony Haygarth and Thomas Craig star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a three-part story. Stuart encourages Stevie to again work undercover as heroin dealer Rob Towler's girlfriend, not realising the chemistry that still exists between them. The case progresses well, especially when Stevie's ex-boss Jim Rutherford turns up to help. Eventually her cover is blown by a member of the gang as the Sun Hill detectives close in for a sting operation, and Stuart is left questioning his colleague's loyalties when she whisks Towler away on her motorbike and disappears without trace. Continues on Monday

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira announces she is leaving Holby, but Greg refuses to let her leave without telling her what she means to him. Eddi cannot wait to get away from Luc and AAU, but a power cut forces the pair to work together in a life-threatening situation. Meanwhile, Hanssen has to think on his feet when asked about future plans for the hospital

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: An anxious Selena learns the identity of her baby's father after Maggie persuades her to have a scan, while Charlie worries Stitch is a bad influence on Guppy. The team and the fire brigade are forced to act when the negligence of two drug addicts puts their child's life at risk. A woman rushes to confront her husband who is having an affair, but gets trapped and has to be rescued by Jeff and Dixie

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The evidence against undercover cop Stevie mounts up, suggesting she has thrown away her career for the love of a villain. Stuart finally catches up with the pair, and his suspicions are confirmed when she turns a gun on her colleague and makes her escape. With the violent Tameside Crew wreaking carnage across Sun Hill in pursuit of Towler, Stuart is suspended from duty for being too close to the case. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant checks on Kathy but she's angry with him for not being honest. Kathy starts drinking alone and asks for waiter Emil to deliver some food. Emil makes a pass at Kathy

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy decides to see the sights of Paris on her own. The trip to France also opens old wounds for Tiffany and Grant. Bianca decides to make the most of her last day in Paris

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Anna is relieved when Gino believes she miscarried, and they spend the night together in the cottage before telling their families of their intention to marry. James sees a psychiatrist who agrees to treat his sexuality, Elizabeth worries that Regina is getting closer to discovering the truth about Sarah's past, and Jack is stunned to learn that he is Anna's father. However, a bigger shock is in store for those at Ash Park when a body is found in the river. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: One of Siegfried's old friends lends a hand during an emergency operation, James helps a client avoid arrest, and Helen plans a wedding anniversary dinner. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Lynda Bellingham star

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A straightforward suicide case takes a sinister turn for Paul when he comes to a bizarre conclusion. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant and Michael Melia

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey's house becomes infested with mice, forcing her to shelve plans for a perfume-selling party. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Spike's prospective father-in-law decides it would be a good idea if he were to join the family business. Comedy, starring Paul Shane and Jeffrey Holland

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth prepares for the Mayor's annual fancy-dress ball, only to have her dream of wearing a regal costume shattered. Fortunately, an important new neighbour could provide the chance to claw back some social credibility. Roy Clarke's comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly scares Billy Ingleton out of his wits by mischievously starting a rumour there is a tiger on the loose. The story spreads like wildfire, leading to unexpected consequences for the practical-joking pensioner. Long-running comedy from the pen of Roy Clarke, starring Frank Thornton and guest Norman Wisdom

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A diabetic girl disappears without her insulin, and Lynley discovers she was last seen talking to a couple who had previously been sighted with a youngster later found drowned in a lake. The detective stages a press appeal, hoping to learn more about the mysterious pair and reveal their roles in both cases. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry celebrates his birthday at a comedy club, where a controversial act comes under fire from a group of hecklers. Sandra discovers the most vocal member of the gang is the niece of a political activist who went missing 25 years previously after waging a campaign against the comedian. Agreeing to reinvestigate the disappearance, the squad discovers that the activist and her boyfriend had infiltrated an extreme right-wing group with links to the dangerous criminal who murdered Jack's wife. Guest starring Phil Daniels, Joe Absolom and John Henshaw

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Lynda La Plante's crime drama. DI Pat North investigates the case of a woman abducted in broad daylight, but a sudden murder confession leaves the squad with more questions than answers. Meanwhile, DCS Michael Walker begins to fear for his children's safety at the hands of his ex-wife's new boyfriend. Kate Buffery and David Hayman star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The evidence against undercover cop Stevie mounts up, suggesting she has thrown away her career for the love of a villain. Stuart finally catches up with the pair, and his suspicions are confirmed when she turns a gun on her colleague and makes her escape. With the violent Tameside Crew wreaking carnage across Sun Hill in pursuit of Towler, Stuart is suspended from duty for being too close to the case. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam is called to a murder at a textiles factory, which he is shocked to discover will be his own home in the 21st century. He puts his knowledge of forensics into use to find the killer, but Gene has a simpler approach - the first suspect to speak did it. As battle lines are drawn across CID, it becomes increasingly clear that the Seventies method of detection may in this case be right. John Simm stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The evidence against undercover cop Stevie mounts up, suggesting she has thrown away her career for the love of a villain. programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira fights her feelings for Greg, but is drawn ever closer to him when they attend a medical conference to secure funding for the Cardiac Trauma Unit. Michael finds himself in a difficult position, and Ric is annoyed when a difficult case on the Keller ward attracts media attention - though Hanssen sees the situation as an opportunity to secure positive publicity for the hospital

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Paramedic Josh is on a plane to Holby when he is forced back into action by an emergency involving a suspected suicide bomber. Selena informs Nathan she is pregnant and the pair make a decision, while Sam notices Tess and Abs are behaving in an unusual manner. Medical drama, starring Ian Bleasdale

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three of three. In a flashback sequence, Stevie reveals to Stuart how she discovered the drug deal was a set-up and why she disobeyed instructions in order to protect her boyfriend. Stuart is astonished that her erratic behaviour was designed to save his life and expose the real villain behind the Tameside Crew. As she makes plans to flee the country, the gang seems always to be two steps ahead of her - and Stevie still has to make the biggest decision of her life

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Tony's enquiries lead to a revelation from an unexpected quarter. Grant and Tiffany find themselves under pressure from Peggy as they try to keep the events of Paris a secret

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca continues to bury her head in the sand. Irene hears some disturbing news about Tony. Tiffany tries her hand as a peace envoy and Peggy is forced to confront some home-truths

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: An elderly Sarah reveals the truth about Bert Ford's death. Meanwhile in 1953, Sarah and Anna start their trial periods in their new homes, and Jack is furious at Carolyn for having kept their daughter a secret. Olivia becomes concerned over James's brutal hospital treatment and Elizabeth makes a shock announcement. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried somehow manages to lose Mrs Pettinger's cat and James tries to dissuade his daughter from becoming a vet. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul faces a dangerous confrontation during a desperate search for an abducted boy whose health is failing. Detective drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon foils a mugging at Chigwell station, and ends up befriending the intended victim - who turns out to be financial adviser to Dorien's husband

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ballroom dancers Barry and Yvonne throw a party in their chalet, inviting only those members of staff they consider socially acceptable - which counts out comic Ted Bovis for a start. Jimmy Perry and David Croft's holiday camp comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc and Jeffrey Holland

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard is forced to think on his feet when he forgets his wedding anniversary and tells Hyacinth he has arranged to have a security alarm fitted as a present. Social-climbing comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley finally finishes his off-the-road motorised scooter, while bored son-in-law Barry longs for a walk on the wild side. Rural comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Thora Hird

programme.title: Inspector Alleyn Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective cancels a planned break in the company of his close friend Agatha Troy after being hauled into the assistant commissioner's office and ordered to investigate the death of a man found clad in monks' robes in a convent. The apparent murder coincides with the theft of a priceless chalice, testing the sleuth's powers of deduction to their full extent. Crime drama, guest starring David Haig, with Patrick Malahide

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The daughter of a man held responsible for a fatal boating accident on the Thames comes forward with new evidence, claiming that a property developer sabotaged the vessel. The case means putting Strickland's wealthy sailing friends under the spotlight, much to the team's delight. Guest starring Barbara Flynn (Cranford)

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: As a third victim is found murdered, Marilyn soon identifies the man responsible as Damon Morton (Iain Glen), and Walker must gather evidence to secure his conviction

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part three of three. In a flashback sequence, Stevie reveals to Stuart how she discovered the drug deal was a set-up and why she disobeyed instructions in order to protect her boyfriend. Stuart is astonished that her erratic behaviour was designed to save his life and expose the real villain behind the Tameside Crew. As she makes plans to flee the country, the gang seems always to be two steps ahead of her - and Stevie still has to make the biggest decision of her life

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam discovers many of his colleagues are taking bribes from a local gangster, and resolves to put a stop to police corruption. However, a skeleton in Gene's cupboard makes him reluctant to join the crusade, and his fears are underlined when a young woman mixed up in the case meets a grisly end. The 21st-century detective also can't resist tracking down his mother, who is facing money worries

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Hanssen discovers Sahira is planning to leave Holby and move away with her husband - so he decides to do everything he can to make her stay. Frieda's nerves about her exam results threaten to get the better of her as she diagnoses a fellow F1 student, while Dan is far from happy at having to work with Chrissie and Sacha following their holiday in Australia

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Sam fails to show up for work, and Tess becomes distressed and angry that she is the last person to know he has gone missing. Selena treats a mother with a spinal injury and argues with Nathan over whether she should keep the baby. Medical drama, starring Suzanne Packer and Elizabeth Carling

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mel's younger brother and his girlfriend come to stay on New Year's Eve and she goes to work thinking the couple are happily celebrating in Trafalgar Square - until Sun Hill officers are called to investigate a stabbing in a club. With the troublesome youngster in the frame for the attack, apparently provoked by a fellow clubber who made advances to his companion, Mel sets out to persuade her colleagues he is innocent

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky is stunned by Bianca's news. Grant tries to make peace with Peggy. Irene discovers her maternal instincts. A face from Tony's past comes back to haunt him

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Irene is still causing friction in Simon and Tony's house. Tiffany receives a call from her dad who is in hospital. Mark gets the all clear for three months after an AIDS examination

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Those involved in Bert's death and its subsequent cover-up search for inner peace. Sarah battles her own demons, but is aware that Colin's suffering is greater. Meanwhile, James' treatment continues to cause him suffering and Jack is determined to find answers after Carolyn's revelation. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried takes matters into his own hands when he discovers who shot war veteran Jacob Pearson's dog, and a farmer is found to be sneaking his cows onto a neighbouring property to enjoy better grazing. Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy star

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paul gets drawn into a case involving a maverick policeman, and becomes doubly determined to move away. As he sets about packing his bags, son Marty gets more than he bargained for while trying to help a homeless girl. Drama, starring Nigel Le Vaillant, Tim Vincent and Tamzin Malleson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon gets a passion for democracy - and decides to buy Darryl's vote from Tracey for the local by-election. Robert Llewellyn guests stars, with Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin ropes the reluctant Yellowcoats into entering the town carnival, and puts Spike in charge of designing their float. Jimmy Perry and David Croft's holiday camp comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc and Jeffrey Holland

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth plans an extravagant riverside picnic and invites all and sundry from the higher echelons of society, including the vicar and his wife. Alas for her dreams of social success, the event proves a wash-out. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly suggests Clegg perform a ritual exorcism on Howard's bicycle when it starts acting like a machine possessed in the presence of Pearl. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Robert Fyfe

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. As Dalziel takes it easy at a local B&B after a bout of ill-health, Pascoe returns to his home town to attend the wedding of an old friend. However, the ceremony ends abruptly when a man is found beaten to death. Andy joins his colleague to investigate - but the duo become rapidly embroiled in a plot of intrigue and murder that implicates Peter's father. Warren Clarke, Colin Buchanan, John McEnery and Jim Carter star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry is forced to confront his past when the team reinvestigates the 33-year-old murder of a doctor whose body was found hanging from a meat hook on a butcher's stall at Smithfield Market. The butcher had gone missing on the same day, and since then the community has rallied to help his widow keep the business afloat. However, his recently discovered remains reveal a match to an unidentified person and lead to a shocking revelation for Sandra. Guest starring Ken Farrington, David Calder and Diana Quick, with Amanda Redman and Alun Armstrong

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Fifteen-year-old Cassie Booth goes missing while doing her local paper round, and her blood-stained clothes are later found in a nearby boathouse owned by Karl Wilding. programme.title: Pobol y Môr
programme.desc: From sunrise to sunset on the stunning Gower peninsula - a day at the seaside in the company of surf photographer Carys, Ramsey Island warden Nia, and Menai Strait mussel-boat fisherman John

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The case of a roadside recovery service that left pensioners stranded. Plus, a report on fake travel companies on social media and tips on environmentally friendly camping

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a con woman's fake claims of having cancer were exposed by her distinctive wallpaper, and the hidden compartment in a car that brought a gang defrauding charities to justice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides. Plus, Anita shows off pieces of pottery with a royal connection

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: It's Valerie's last day at the practice and she is planning to go out in style. Thanks to Ruhma, the Mill is alive with the sound of music

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Kamran, Abi, Livi and Nigel vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley heads on a house-hunting mission in rural Devon for a couple who are looking for their first home together, with a budget of £525,000

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team transforms a Reading garden to make it safe for children - despite the fact that no landscaper has been willing to take on the broken slate and rotten decking

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people to the room, with a lazy Susan, Italian jewellery, a cigar case and a ship's wheel on offer to the five professional dealers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben confronts Whitney about spending time with Callum but she tells him to talk to his husband instead. Rocky's interest in the local houses leaves Sonia suspicious, Bobby and Dana take a big step in their relationship and Lola worries about Isaac's erratic behaviour and obsession with Paul

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Fletch's relationship with Evie grows strained as decisions are made about Andrei's future, and he finds himself forced to break her heart to protect his daughter. Ange and Josh try to avoid each other, but must learn to put personal issues aside when a patient's life hangs in the balance. Overwhelmed with guilt, Jeong confesses his mistake with the drugs cupboard to Dom

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Optics expert Richard Biggs works on a home-made telescope, built by the owner's grandfather from bits of scrap during the Second World War. Saddler Suzie Fletcher is brought a leather briefcase once owned by an intrepid traveller, while furniture restorer Will Kirk works his magic on a vintage writing bureau. The final challenge sees Dominic Chinea reviving a rusty child's seesaw that dates from the 1930s and was ahead of its time - not only did it go up and down, but it also spun round. Can Dominic get it spinning again?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team dives into the case of surf-school owner Jake Peters' murder. The victim was shot in his workshop and Humphrey suspects there is more to the incident than meets the eye when he spots the door was locked from the inside with no other obvious means of entry. Suspicion falls on three pupils who have their own agendas, while grieving wife Katie and best friend Karl Slater are harbouring dark secrets. Away from the workplace, Humphrey continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille and decides to take matters into his own hands when faced with competition for her affections. Caribbean-set detective drama, with guest appearances from Hetti Bywater, Claire Goose and Will Mellor

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England and Croatia v Scotland. Action from the final Group D fixtures, which took place at Wembley Stadium and Hampden Park respectively. Gareth Southgate's side were beaten by the Czechs in qualifying for this tournament, a result which ended the Three Lions' 43-game unbeaten run in qualifiers. Scotland had never lost to Croatia in five previous meetings, and manager Steve Clarke will have been hoping for a repeat of their most recent encounter - a 2-0 home win for the Scots in 2013

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to present a countdown of the best midfielders to play in the European Championships

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Romero's newly stable, blissful life is placed in jeopardy by his latest investigation. Meanwhile, Norman considers holding back certain key facts from Dr Edwards. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga and Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman is forced to confront his inner demons as his sessions with Dr Edwards progress. Meanwhile, Norma tries to keep her own problems hidden

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England. A chance to see the final Group D fixture for both sides, which took place at Wembley Stadium. With commentary from Steve Wilson and Danny Murphy

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The progress of a three-bedroom semi in Crewe, Cheshire, a studio apartment in London's Hammersmith and a mid-terrace in Colne, Lancashire - all of which are bought as investments

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: A report on the cyclists who found out their insurance did not fully cover for theft or accidents when they went holiday, and a look at a villa rental company that conned customers

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: CCTV footage from a petrol station helps convict a gang of cold-blooded killers. Elsewhere, a criminal's mobile phone finally uncovers a gang targeting the vulnerable

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

Along with their bandmates Candida Doyle and Rowetta Satchell, they join experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey scouring Kent Showground for items that will make them the most profit for charity. While they're busy, presenter Charlie Ross pays a visit to a recording studio in Wales, Rockfields

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie's search for adventure brings her to a magical place called 'Wonderland'. But whether she will like what she finds remains to be seen. Drama, starring Sarah Moyle

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Cole, Charlie, Karen and Charlotte

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a house-hunting mission with a dynamic couple in the Herefordshire countryside, hoping they fall in love with the homes they see for their £565,000 budget. Along the way, the presenter heads to the banks of the River Wye to gets to grips with paddle boarding, an ever growing low-impact water sport

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers Harry and David Rich set to work transforming a garden in Bitterne, near Southampton, for Katie and Christian and their two children, Max and Olivia

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including a collection of printing plates, a retro Olivetti typewriter and a toy steam engine. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Portugal v France (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from both sides' final Group F match, which comes from Puskas Arena in Budapest. This game is a repeat of the Euro 2016 final, when Portugal overcame hosts France to lift their first-ever major trophy. This time around, both sides have experienced mixed fortunes so far, with the Portuguese leaving it late to beat Hungary 3-0, before suffering a 4-2 defeat at the hands of Germany, while Didier Deschamps' men won 1-0 against the Germans, but were then surprisingly held to a 1-1 draw by the Hungarians, leaving it all to play for in this group. The winners of this pool will head to Bucharest for their last-16 match, while the runners-up will be in action at Wembley Stadium on Tuesday evening. Commentary comes from Guy Mowbray and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Peter Crouch, Maya Jama and Alex Horne offer their own unique take on the tournament, including reactions to Germany v Hungary and Portugal v France with celebrity guests and a few surprises

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards discuss the finest goalscorers in the history of the European Championships

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A full-match rerun of the latest game

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore a large house in Penge, London, and two terraces in Silverdale, Staffordshire, and Gunhill, Coventry, before they are sold at auction

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville focus on the devastating impact of coronavirus on travel, including the insurance company that refused to pay out after a couple's grand tour of South America came to a grinding halt. They also speak to a customer who was forced to turn to local police for help after her online travel agency failed to answer its 24-hour emergency helpline

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a simple business card revealed the true identities of a gang of con artists, and a ripped-up note that held the key to locking away two murderers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined by experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram as two teams visit the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, scouring an antiques fair to pick up bargains. Eric also takes a little time out to travel across the border to Cirencester, Gloucestershire, to find some Roman treasures.

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: While Daniel and Bear's concern grows for Sid, Jimmi is delighted to welcome a travelling friend who has come to spread some joy. Local bad boys Aaron and Lenny have big plans for the day, but don't anticipate meeting Rob and Harriet who are determined to stop them

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Candice, Esther, Craig and Shaun vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a rural property-shopping couple with an £850,000 budget put down roots closer to family in Somerset. Along the way, Jonnie visits Glastonbury and learns how its religious past and strong links to English legend still resonate with people there today

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Harry and Anna want the garden of the house they share in Romsey, Hampshire, to be less about raised beds and more about raised cocktail glasses. However, their garden is currently a boring, blank canvas. To realise their dreams of a stylish social garden, the duo call upon the services of Charlie Dimmock and the David and Harry Rich to perform the transformation they need on a budget of £4,000

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Dealers Ian Humphries, Jane Cave, Adrian Higham, James Gooch, Natasha Francis and Tracy Martin go head to head over a polychrome carving of St Sebastian, a pair of vintage Bakelite phones, some Chinese fortune sticks and a piece of rock 'n' roll history. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The discovery of a double crime prompts Sonia to point the finger of suspicion at Rocky. Bobby decides to come clean to Dana, while Ruby and Martin receive another setback. Suki is furious with Kheerat, so he tells Sharon it's over for the sake of his family. But Suki's anger turns to upset when she hears some shocking news

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea lands herself in hot water with the police, and when Jack suggests she gets herself a lawyer, she asks Gray out for lunch - and it isn't long before the two are flirting. Isaac's obsession continues to build, Ben opens up to Callum and Peter encourages Bobby to fight for Dana

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Simon Day, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Henning Wehn join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Theo Paphitis steps in for Peter Jones, joining regular Dragons Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies to assess more money-making schemes. A musical inventor shows off his drumming training device, and four children make a first aid demonstration to secure investment for their parents' business. Plus, pitches for culinary alcohol and a prototype shoe mechanism

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Heanor in Derbyshire, where she discovers a rare vase. With experts Thomas Forrester and Catherine Southon

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don turns his attention to containers, planting them up for drama and summer colour in the jewel garden and showing us how to re-pot citrus plants. In Hampshire, Joe Swift meets the designer of an award-winning contemporary garden to explore the techniques and the plants she has used to seamlessly integrate both house and garden

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Warrington Wolves, Huddersfield Giants v Salford Red Devils and Catalans Dragons v Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of day two from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club, featuring second-round matches. Karolina Pliskova was victorious when this competition was last stage in 2019 and she got her tournament under way at this stage with a routine 6-3, 6-3 victory over Margarita Gasparyan. British number one Johanna Konta also booked her place in the third round when she prevailed 6-4, 7-6 against Maria Sakkari

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team values antiques at Guildford Cathedral, where James Lewis discovers a brass figure that could be six centuries old, and Catherine Southon examines a pocket watch with a difference. Paul Martin takes time out to look at the world's first motor-racing track, and goes for a spin in a 1930s sports car

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journey brings him to the Italian island of Sicily. In the capital city of Palermo, he takes in the art and architecture of the Futurists and feasts on spaghetti and sardines in the city's Ballaro market. Then, he makes his way through the ancient town of Agrigento and the port of Siracus

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: John Cooper Clarke and Phill Jupitus embark on the cross-country treasure-hunting challenge in East Anglia, as well as taking a look at the Norwich School of 19th-century artists

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon presents a home makeover show with a difference, as cutting-edge virtual reality and visual effects enable people to see what the future of their home could look like, before it is built in reality. In the first edition, two architects compete to remodel a home in Scotland for 34-year-old Andy and his 32-year-old partner Esther, whose three-bedroom house in Stirling is now too cramped for the pair and their three small children. But which of the two radical new designs will then be built in real life?

programme.title: The Countess and the Russian Billionaire
programme.desc: An inside look at the secret world of Sergei Pugachev and his British partner Countess Alexandra Tolstoy. A hugely rich and powerful couple, with three beautiful children, homes across the world, and unimaginable wealth, the couple appeared to have it all, but their fairy tale life is about to come crashing down around them

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig invites Aisling Bea, Corey Taylor and Ross Noble and Alan Davies to answer questions on subjects associated with nosey and noisy. Since the questions are impossible to answer, points are awarded for being interesting rather than correct

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: As news of the Covid-19 virus broke around the world, teams of scientists jumped into action to create a vaccine against a virus that no one had heard of before. This programme looks at the extraordinary inside story of the unprecedented quest to develop vaccines to fight Covid-19, with unique access to five teams of scientists around the globe. Narrated by Lesley Sharp

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part one of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first Northern Ireland parliament. Fearful for their lives, they had come to a city scarred by bitter sectarian violence. One hundred years on, this is the story of the dramatic events that led to the partition of Ireland - one that continues to reverberate to the present day

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part two of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first parliament of Northern Ireland. The King's visit to Belfast was the culmination of three centuries of history. This concluding film examines how political tensions and outbreaks of sectarian violence threatened to destabilise the new Northern Irish state

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works with one of the UK's biggest water companies. He traces the flow of this precious resource from the reservoir to homes and businesses, before finding out what happens with wastewater at the other end of the system

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Featuring an island paradise for puffins in Scotland and golden hares in Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, Chris Packham checks in on the live nests, and Michaela Strachan reports on the plight of the Norfolk plovers

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of day three from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club, featuring third-round matches. British involvement ended at this stage when the tournament was last held in 2019, as Johanna Konta lost 3-6, 2-6 against Tunisia's Ons Jabeur. Carolina Wozniacki's run also came to an end in round three when she was beaten in three sets by Aryna Sabalenka

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team value antiques in Sheffield, where experts Thomas Plant and James Lewis have their work cut out for them. A set of watercolours of the city appeals to Thomas, while James finds a Victorian automaton that causes a stir at auction. Plus, Paul takes time out to explore the story of the Great Exhibition of 1851

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey around the island of Sicily, heading inland from Agrigento towards the picturesque but remote village of Gangi. Here, he meets a local historian and traces the story behind this notorious mafia hideout of the 1930s. Later, Michael concludes his Sicilian journey on the circular railway around Mount Etna

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On one of the hottest days of the year, the Dover RNLI crew race to a stricken Channel swimmer enduring difficulties within eyesight of the French coast. In Mudeford, there are growing fears for a dinghy sailor four miles out in rough seas and refusing to accept help. And the Tenby team are called to a runaway yacht which is on the verge of running aground

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Germany v Hungary (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the third and final Group F encounter for both sides, which takes place at Fußball Arena München. The Germans bounced back from a 1-0 defeat at the hands of France in style, overcoming holders Portugal 4-2 in arguably the game of the tournament so far. Meanwhile, the Hungarians have just one point to their name, but it was a memorable one, as they held world champions France to a 1-1 draw in front of their own fans, and they will now look to produce an even more unlikely result, as only a win would be good enough for them to progress to the next round. Commentary comes from Steve Bower and Robbie Savage

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Action from the first T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Sophia Gardens in Cardiff. These nations have only faced each other on nine previous occasions in this format of the game, with England winning five of those matches, and Sri Lanka triumphing in the other four

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole and Mairi prove that they are some of the best bargain hunters, as they create a new bargain border in Beechgrove for the price of a little work. It's an allotment special this week. and Calum Clunie is in his Leven allotment, where he is growing for upcoming shows and this week, he proves that he really knows his oversized onions

programme.title: Inside Culture
programme.desc: In a special programme, Mary Beard is in conversation with critically acclaimed and internationally bestselling Nigerian author Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie. Her latest book, Notes on Grief, conveys her startlingly powerful reaction to losing her father last year, the man she credits with instilling in her a strong feminist instinct. Her writing is both tender and fierce, whether it's her Women's Prize-winning novel Half of a Yellow Sun or her influential essay We Should All Be Feminists, and she's often named as one of Africa's best writers. In her first public appearance since the news of her mother's death, she talks to Mary about the power of literature to heal, how it feels to have Beyonce using her words in her songs and why Enid Blyton was her inspiration to write as she was growing up in Nigeria

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: A fire that kills a man and could have led to more deaths appears to be arson and the culprit is likely to strike again - but identifying the criminal proves tricky as all the evidence was destroyed in the blaze. Meanwhile, a neighbourhood team gathers enough intelligence to raid the address of a local suspected drug dealer, and paramedics are prevented from carrying out CPR

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: There's theft in the Scottish seas - but do great skuas deserve their reputation as pirates of the skies? Megan McCubbin goes to the Welsh border to investigate why restoring peatland bog is so important for wildlife. There's also a live round-up of the wild soap operas playing out in Norfolk, and a peek at a secretive owl in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Tennis: Eastbourne
programme.desc: The Viking International Eastbourne. Clare Balding presents coverage of day four from Devonshire Park Lawn Tennis Club, featuring the quarter-finals. Ons Jabeur, Angelique Kerber, Kiki Bertens and Karolina Pliskova were the victorious players at this stage when the tournament was last held in 2019, defeating Alize Cornet, Simona Halep, Aryna Sabalenka and Ekaterina Alexandrova respectively. With commentary and analysis from Annabel Croft, Anne Keothavong, Sam Smith and Gigi Salmon

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team visits Manchester, where Caroline Hawley inspects an autograph from a rock 'n' roll legend, and Anita Manning values a concertina. Presenter Paul Martin learns about the city's musical heritage, from the Halle Orchestra to New Order and Oasis

programme.title: Live T20 Cricket
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Isa Guha presents coverage of the second game in the three-match series, which comes from Sophia Gardens in Cardiff. Prior to the start of this series, the last meeting between the sides in this format was in Colombo in October 2018, when opener Jason Roy's impressive 69 off 36 balls and Joe Denly's bowling figures of 4-19 helped England to a 30-run victory. They will be hopeful of another strong performance this evening on home soil given they are currently at the top of the ICC Twenty20 rankings, with the Sri Lankans down in eighth place. With commentary from Michael Vaughan, Phil Tufnell, Jimmy Anderson, Ebony Rainford-Brent and Andy Zaltzman

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A selection of highlights from the stand-up show focusing on a different topic each week. First up are John Bishop, Sarah Millican, Phil Wang, Ellie Taylor, Ed Byrne, Angela Barnes, Gary Delaney, Tom Stade and Laura Lexx giving their thoughts on relationships

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Katie Razzall

programme.title: The Edge (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about England's Test cricket team between 2009 and 2013, the first and only English side to ever reach the top of the world rankings. Featuring contributions from Andrew Strauss, Kevin Pietersen, Alastair Cook, James Anderson, Stuart Broad, Monty Panesar, Graeme Swann, Jonathan Trott, Matt Prior, Ian Bell, Paul Collingwood, Steven Finn, Tim Bresnan, David Saker and Andy Flower

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett hosts the semi-final, as the four remaining sewers go back in time to the 1940s. Attempting to revive both the glamorous and thrifty styles of the decade, they tackle the decade's most elegant and innovative garments. First up, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to make the decade's most famous trousers, Oxford bags. For the Transformation Challenge, the sewers are set a wartime task to turn old parachutes into glamorous dresses. In the Made to Measure challenge, the sewers take on their final icon of 40s fashion, Dior's New Look. Characterised by an hourglass silhouette and involving masses of fabric, the sewers must exaggerate their model's figure to create the iconic shape

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers Ben and Myles turn detective to track down the assets owned by a tricky debtor whose car dealership owes thousands of pounds to an unhappy customer. Luke and Grant have an unusual job to do at a pizza shop when they find an unexpected squatter inside

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins workers at the Port of Tyne, helping to unload cargo containers from the quayside, and discovering how their contents are transported around the country

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a blue moon, with breathtaking skies, bending palms and waves that accentuate the cool night breeze

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: As the cruise ship Galaxy docks in Jamaica for Christmas, the 2,000 passengers look forward to a sun-drenched festive season, but it's business as usual for the crew. Meanwhile, singer Jane is reunited with her Danish boyfriend and social hostess Amanda also manages to keep smiling _ despite feeling distinctly under the weather

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: Hacker and Sir Humphrey are summoned to appear before a select committee investigating charges of waste in the ministry, where they frantically pass the buck to protect themselves and each other. Starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: DeVere decides to shoot a commercial for his supermarket empire at Grantleigh and Audrey takes advantage of the cameras to tell the world what she thinks of him. Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles star

programme.title: The Jet Stream and Us
programme.desc: Documentary examining the influence that the jet stream - a ribbon of fast-moving air high up in the atmosphere - has on everyday weather. Here, scientists explain the effects the phenomenon has had on climate and discuss what could happen should it change its pattern of behaviour

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: Documentary following veteran civil rights lawyer-turned-district attorney Larry Krasner as he adopts a radical approach to criminal justice in Philadelphia

programme.title: Philly DA: Breaking the Law
programme.desc: Newly elected DA Larry Krasner and his team begin a radical overhaul of Philadelphia's criminal justice system from within by poring over a cache of police misconduct archives

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon charts the history of American art on a coast-to-coast journey, beginning in the east, where he discusses how the country's rise to power was underpinned by the destruction of Native American cultural heritage. He examines portraits of Puritan settlers in Massachusetts, tells the story of a famous painting of Quaker settler William Penn, reads one of the world's most expensive books in Philadelphia and explore the wilderness landscapes that inspired artist Thomas Cole

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a blue moon, with breathtaking skies, bending palms and waves that accentuate the cool night breeze

programme.title: The Cruise

Meanwhile, singer Jane is reunited with her Danish boyfriend and social hostess Amanda also manages to keep smiling _ despite feeling distinctly under the weather

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: It is Mother's Day and Julia is feeling completely ignored, while Meg's family have gone way over the top in reaction to her cancer diagnosis. Anne is beside herself with excitement at the prospect of a day out with her Mammy, while Liz and Kevin try to avoid mum stuff altogether with a trip to the countryside. Amanda's mother Felicity travels from Cheltenham for a gloriously passive-aggressive celebratory lunch. Guest starring Joanna Lumley

programme.title: The Jet Stream and Us
programme.desc: Documentary examining the influence that the jet stream - a ribbon of fast-moving air high up in the atmosphere - has on everyday weather. Here, scientists explain the effects the phenomenon has had on climate and discuss what could happen should it change its pattern of behaviour

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a stream trickling through the depths of the forest

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: The crew gets into the swing of New Year's Eve celebrations and, as the countdown to midnight begins, singer Jane McDonald and DJ Scotty help ensure the evening goes with a bang. Meanwhile, a veteran passenger realises she's on the wrong ship and a stowaway is discovered

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Between 1948 and 1960, governments around the world invested in electric tracks and diesel, but in Britain hundreds of steam-powered trains were built. This film looks at the reasons behind their continued production, and asks what happened to them

programme.title: The Flying Scotsman: A Rail Romance
programme.desc: To celebrate its 90th birthday, Barbara Flynn narrates the story of the nation's love affair with the Flying Scotsman - arguably the most famous locomotive in the world, and one that symbolises all that was great about British engineering. In 2004, public donations and grants were used to purchase the train and keep it in Britain, where the National Railway Museum has been working to restore it to its former glory. First shown in 2013

programme.title: King Arthur's Britain: The Truth Unearthed
programme.desc: With access to a major new excavation on the Tintagel peninsula in Cornwall, Professor Alice Roberts pulls together the latest evidence to reveal what Dark Age Britain was really like. Long known to have been a Dark Ages settlement, the new evidence reveals that Tintagel was also a seat of power, and Alice explores the link between the location and Arthurian legend, tracking down the early sources for the period and the first written reference to King Arthur

programme.title: The Making of King Arthur
programme.desc: In the years popularly associated with King Arthur's reign over medieval England, Norman forces came to occupy the land, prompting oppressed locals to romanticise their history. Simon Armitage reveals how these formative years helped develop belief in the monarch as the Once and Future King, and explores the effect the Arthurian tales have had on the national consciousness of Great Britain

programme.title: Handmade in Japan
programme.desc: Documentary about traditional Japanese craftspeople, beginning by following Shiro Kunimitsu and his family as they craft a katana of exceptional sharpness and durability, continuing a tradition begun by their ancestors nearly three centuries ago

programme.desc: Alan Yentob travels to Hollywood to interview Mel Brooks, the comedy actor, writer, producer and director who turned 91 last year. At the time of filming, the musical theatre version of Brooks' 1974 comedy horror Young Frankenstein had just opened on London's West End. Yentob marks the occasion by profiling its creator, observing him at work and play, and revisiting archive interviews stretching back decades

When DM realises that the day is being stolen and replaced with a silent night, he enlists the help of expert colleagues - the night shift! programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.desc: Love Monster has been chosen as the prize-picker at the Harvest Day contest, but when he gets there it turns out there is only one prize and three teams. This means he has to choose a winner between his friends - Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and the In-Chicks. Round one is fancy fashion foodies, and everyone loves Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, so Love Monster thinks he can just pick her. Then it is round two - the veggie variety show. Everyone loves the In-Chicks, so Love Monster now has to choose between them and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny. Then it is the final round, which is the tantalizing taste test of home grown goodies, and this time everyone loves Bad Idea Puppy. Love Monster has no idea how he can pick a winner and runs off and hides, but then he realises Bad Idea Puppy always makes him laugh, so he declares him the winner.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Elder Panda is teaching Love Monster to fly a kite. Love Monster loves to watch it swish around in the air. Elder Panda goes off to get some froyo, which is perfect on Kite Day. Before he goes, he tells Love Monster that soon it will be time for him to go on to his next kiting challenge. Love Monster looks at all the other kites and sees the dragon kite, which is the largest kite of them all. Love Monster’s small kite doesn’t seem that exciting any more. He decides to try the dragon kite for just a minute. At first it seems easy, but then a wind gets it tangled in a tree. The rope breaks, and the kite flies off, causing mayhem in Fluffytown with everyone thinking it is a real dragon invasion. Finally, Elder Panda arrives and manages to bring it down to the ground using an umbrella, and Love Monster comes clean, saying how sorry he is. Elder Panda is disappointed but he does understand, as he too wanted to fly before he could run, but sometimes you have to start small before going big.

Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny arrives, and it is soon clear they do things very differently. Love Monster has a night-time hot chocolate, while Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny drinks cold carrot juice. She brushes her teeth, and he flosses. Love Monster likes a bedtime story, while Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny prefers music. This confuses Love Monster - he thought everyone does the same things at bedtime. Then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny starts snoring and talking in her sleep and grabbing all the covers. They both end up on the floor and decide there isn't room for both of them on the snooze-cloud. Love Monster goes to his bedroom, and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny stays in the tent. Overwhelmed with guilt, Jeong confesses his mistake with the drugs cupboard to Dom

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Optics expert Richard Biggs works on a home-made telescope, built by the owner's grandfather from bits of scrap during the Second World War. Saddler Suzie Fletcher is brought a leather briefcase once owned by an intrepid traveller, while furniture restorer Will Kirk works his magic on a vintage writing bureau. The final challenge sees Dominic Chinea reviving a rusty child's seesaw that dates from the 1930s and was ahead of its time - not only did it go up and down, but it also spun round. Can Dominic get it spinning again?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team dives into the case of surf-school owner Jake Peters' murder. The victim was shot in his workshop and Humphrey suspects there is more to the incident than meets the eye when he spots the door was locked from the inside with no other obvious means of entry. Suspicion falls on three pupils who have their own agendas, while grieving wife Katie and best friend Karl Slater are harbouring dark secrets. Away from the workplace, Humphrey continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille and decides to take matters into his own hands when faced with competition for her affections. Caribbean-set detective drama, with guest appearances from Hetti Bywater, Claire Goose and Will Mellor

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England and Croatia v Scotland. Action from the final Group D fixtures, which took place at Wembley Stadium and Hampden Park respectively. Gareth Southgate's side were beaten by the Czechs in qualifying for this tournament, a result which ended the Three Lions' 43-game unbeaten run in qualifiers. Scotland had never lost to Croatia in five previous meetings, and manager Steve Clarke will have been hoping for a repeat of their most recent encounter - a 2-0 home win for the Scots in 2013

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Portugal v France (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from both sides' final Group F match, which comes from Puskas Arena in Budapest. This game is a repeat of the Euro 2016 final, when Portugal overcame hosts France to lift their first-ever major trophy. This time around, both sides have experienced mixed fortunes so far, with the Portuguese leaving it late to beat Hungary 3-0, before suffering a 4-2 defeat at the hands of Germany, while Didier Deschamps' men won 1-0 against the Germans, but were then surprisingly held to a 1-1 draw by the Hungarians, leaving it all to play for in this group. The winners of this pool will head to Bucharest for their last-16 match, while the runners-up will be in action at Wembley Stadium on Tuesday evening. Commentary comes from Guy Mowbray and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Dylan gets cold feet when the time comes to escape Shieldinch, but Cameron's sinister plans continue behind his back. Ash tries to build broken bridges, Rory returns home after his operation and Jac struggles to come to terms with a horrible truth

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Fletch's relationship with Evie grows strained as decisions are made about Andrei's future, and he finds himself forced to break her heart to protect his daughter. Ange and Josh try to avoid each other, but must learn to put personal issues aside when a patient's life hangs in the balance. Overwhelmed with guilt, Jeong confesses his mistake with the drugs cupboard to Dom

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team dives into the case of surf-school owner Jake Peters' murder. The victim was shot in his workshop and Humphrey suspects there is more to the incident than meets the eye when he spots the door was locked from the inside with no other obvious means of entry. Suspicion falls on three pupils who have their own agendas, while grieving wife Katie and best friend Karl Slater are harbouring dark secrets. Away from the workplace, Humphrey continues to struggle with his feelings for Camille and decides to take matters into his own hands when faced with competition for her affections. Caribbean-set detective drama, with guest appearances from Hetti Bywater, Claire Goose and Will Mellor

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Portugal v France (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from both sides' final Group F match, which comes from Puskas Arena in Budapest. This game is a repeat of the Euro 2016 final, when Portugal overcame hosts France to lift their first-ever major trophy. This time around, both sides have experienced mixed fortunes so far, with the Portuguese leaving it late to beat Hungary 3-0, before suffering a 4-2 defeat at the hands of Germany, while Didier Deschamps' men won 1-0 against the Germans, but were then surprisingly held to a 1-1 draw by the Hungarians, leaving it all to play for in this group. The winners of this pool will head to Bucharest for their last-16 match, while the runners-up will be in action at Wembley Stadium on Tuesday evening. Commentary comes from Guy Mowbray and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public sort the treasures from their trash, where one of the dealers is kept on their toes by one of the youngest sellers the den has ever seen

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five pub quizzers from Basingstoke answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Alan, Fred, Kathryn and Lucy pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final Group D fixture for both sides, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. The two sides are familiar with each other, having been drawn in the same qualifying group for this tournament, and the game on this ground was a resounding 5-0 victory for Gareth Southgate's men, with Raheem Sterling claiming a hat-trick. However, while they have four points so far, England have not yet been firing on all cylinders in this tournament, scoring one against Croatia and ending goalless against Scotland. Meanwhile, the Czechs were 2-0 victors over the Scots in their opener, before being held to a 1-1 draw by the Croats, and the competitive nature of this group means it is all to play for going into the final round of fixtures, with four points likely, but not certain, for a team to reach the knockout stage as a best third-place team. With analysis from Roy Keane, Gary Neville, Ian Wright, Reshmin Chowdhury, Ashley Co

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: To celebrate 70 years in the film industry, the actress sits down with Piers for a special in which she covers everything from her time as a young starlet appearing alongside Diana Dors and Bette Davis to her reign as Dynasty legend Alexis Carrington. She tells Piers what she really thought of her famous co-stars and ex-husbands, lifts the lid on her tumultuous affair with Warren Beatty and relives the day she discovered her sister Jackie was terminally ill

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Neil Spooner and Tom Dove travel to Thailand, where they have previously caught huge Siamese carp, but this time the focus is on the other species of carp found there. Their adventure is split between Fishing Park Samui and Gillhams Fishing Resorts, where they will have to use all of their skill and knowledge to outwit as many different carp species as possible, and in the process they will be hoping to bank one of the stunning Jullien's golden carp that can be found there

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Emily, Roddy, Dannii, Morgan, Josh, Tess, Faye, Andy and Lewis are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Two more of the most thrilling and nerve-racking endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Slovakia v Spain (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final match in Group E for both countries, which takes place at Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. These nations last met during qualifying for Euro 2016, sharing one win each. Slovakia recorded a famous 2-1 victory in Zilina in October 2014, but Spain gained a measure of revenge in the return fixture, with Jordi Alba and Andres Iniesta finding the back of the net. While Spain have been based in Seville for disappointing draws with Sweden and Poland, Slovakia have had to uproot from Russia, where they defeated the Poles, before losing to the Swedes. Gary Neville, Ashley Cole and Eniola Aluko provide the expert analysis, with commentary from Clive Tyldesley and Ally McCoist

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: The Chase

They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final Group D fixture for both sides, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. The two sides are familiar with each other, having been drawn in the same qualifying group for this tournament, and the game on this ground was a resounding 5-0 victory for Gareth Southgate's men, with Raheem Sterling claiming a hat-trick. However, while they have four points so far, England have not yet been firing on all cylinders in this tournament, scoring one against Croatia and ending goalless against Scotland. Meanwhile, the Czechs were 2-0 victors over the Scots in their opener, before being held to a 1-1 draw by the Croats, and the competitive nature of this group means it is all to play for going into the final round of fixtures, with four points likely, but not certain, for a team to reach the knockout stage as a best third-place team. With analysis from Roy Keane, Gary Neville, Ian Wright, Reshmin Chowdhury, Ashley Co

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: To celebrate 70 years in the film industry, the actress sits down with Piers for a special in which she covers everything from her time as a young starlet appearing alongside Diana Dors and Bette Davis to her reign as Dynasty legend Alexis Carrington. She tells Piers what she really thought of her famous co-stars and ex-husbands, lifts the lid on her tumultuous affair with Warren Beatty and relives the day she discovered her sister Jackie was terminally ill

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Neil Spooner and Tom Dove travel to Thailand, where they have previously caught huge Siamese carp, but this time the focus is on the other species of carp found there. Their adventure is split between Fishing Park Samui and Gillhams Fishing Resorts, where they will have to use all of their skill and knowledge to outwit as many different carp species as possible, and in the process they will be hoping to bank one of the stunning Jullien's golden carp that can be found there

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Slovakia v Spain (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final match in Group E for both countries, which takes place at Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. These nations last met during qualifying for Euro 2016, sharing one win each. Slovakia recorded a famous 2-1 victory in Zilina in October 2014, but Spain gained a measure of revenge in the return fixture, with Jordi Alba and Andres Iniesta finding the back of the net. While Spain have been based in Seville for disappointing draws with Sweden and Poland, Slovakia have had to uproot from Russia, where they defeated the Poles, before losing to the Swedes. Gary Neville, Ashley Cole and Eniola Aluko provide the expert analysis, with commentary from Clive Tyldesley and Ally McCoist

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Czech Republic v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the final Group D fixture for both sides, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. The two sides are familiar with each other, having been drawn in the same qualifying group for this tournament, and the game on this ground was a resounding 5-0 victory for Gareth Southgate's men, with Raheem Sterling claiming a hat-trick. However, while they have four points so far, England have not yet been firing on all cylinders in this tournament, scoring one against Croatia and ending goalless against Scotland. Meanwhile, the Czechs were 2-0 victors over the Scots in their opener, before being held to a 1-1 draw by the Croats, and the competitive nature of this group means it is all to play for going into the final round of fixtures, with four points likely, but not certain, for a team to reach the knockout stage as a best third-place team. With analysis from Roy Keane, Gary Neville, Ian Wright, Reshmin Chowdhury, Ashley Co

programme.desc: Coma victim Harriet Farmer finally wakes up a year after she was attacked while trying to elope, but her boyfriend Finn Robson is still missing.
