Meanwhile, Amy plays Penny and Bernadette off against each other while the tension brews over Penny's new job. Comedy, guest starring Regina King (Southland), with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Adam passes on a family ring to convey his feelings for Dana, Beverly has difficulty accepting her son has such deep emotions at his tender age. Problems ensue when Lainey and Barry convince Murray to watch American football with Lainey's father. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Murray takes Erica's car privileges away, she and Barry buy a shabby second-hand van to get back at him. Meanwhile, when Adam objects to being voted 'nicest guy' in the school yearbook, he gets a suspension

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake is assigned to a minor graffiti case by Captain Holt as punishment for turning up late for work, but the investigation becomes a major headache when the vandal's identity is revealed. Meanwhile, Gina's psychic friend Carlene visits the precinct to help with a case, and upsets Charles with a prediction about his romantic life. American comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: For the first time in his career, Jake has a growing number of cases he just can't seem to solve, and the other detectives worry his run of bad luck will rub off on them. Holt asks Amy to take charge of a community outreach programme for at-risk youths, and Boyle helps Jeffords with a tough personal assignment. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Andre Braugher

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is furious when she learns that Sheldon has shared intimate details of their relationship with his colleagues at the university, prompting their first fight since moving in together. Raj considers trying to live without financial support from his father, who thinks he is spoilt

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Cut off from his father's fortune, Raj struggles to balance his books, so decides to appoint Sheldon to organise his finances and get him out of debt. Penny considers joining the guys at Comic-Con because she thinks Leonard wants her to come along

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Amy are forced to take an unexpected road trip to Arizona to pick up Sheldon after his epic railway journey ends in chaos. Meanwhile, Howard finds Stuart's relationship with his mother strange, and Penny has an interview for a sales job at Bernadette's workplace. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is forced to teach a course at the university, and Howard surprises the gang when he decides to enrol on it. Meanwhile, Amy plays Penny and Bernadette off against each other while the tension brews over Penny's new job. Comedy, guest starring Regina King (Southland), with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Hoping that Pops' good luck can rub off on him, Adam takes to gambling at school - but ends up losing his toys. Erica wants to take part in a popular TV dance show, but when she gets the chance her dream quickly turns into a nightmare

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and Beverly decide to create a music video to help Erica's application to Julliard stand out, but the production does not go as planned. When Murray bonds with Lainey's father Bill, he learns what it means to be a real friend. Tim Meadows and David Koechner guest star

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: When Rainbow makes a new friend at work who is having problems with her ex-husband, Dre realises that his wife has 'broken bird syndrome'. Andre Jr transforms his shared room with Jack into a frat house, so Ruby and Diane gang up against them

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: The Johnsons follow their tradition of going to the cinema at Christmas but can't agree what to watch: a film about the life of Rosa Parks or the latest superhero blockbuster. Andre Jr is embarrassed about taking a gap year when he bumps into his ex-girlfriend Megan

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta tries to make a deal with a suspect Diaz has just arrested in an attempt to catch a car thief he has been tracking for years. Boyle's colleagues set out to meet his every need when he returns to work after being shot in the line of duty, and Captain Holt struggles to find a home for two puppies

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Santiago's ongoing bet about who can make the most arrests comes to an embarrassing conclusion, while Boyle receives a medal following the shooting, but his pain medication loosens his tongue and he starts to reveal what he really thinks of his colleagues

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Raj turn to Bernadette for advice about their jealousy when Amy and Howard decide to collaborate on a new project. Penny and Leonard learn some new ways to manage Sheldon's irksome behaviour from an unlikely source

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon competes with Wil Wheaton (guest starring as himself) for the role of the new Professor Proton when the scientist's old TV series gets a reboot. Penny looks after Halley when Howard and Bernadette are both put on bed rest

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment consultant Adam is the fourth to host in and around Essex, and hopes to exceed expectations with a menu of posh nosh at his dad's house

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for West Babylon, New York, to help the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts. The chef struggles to cope with disorganised staff and an unappealing menu, but quickly realises the real problem lies in the kitchen, where a lack of leadership and communication is causing serious trouble

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Two daters returning to the restaurant include recruitment consultant Jack, 27, who is unwilling to change for anyone. Will he measure up for 21-year-old hairdresser Shauna? Meanwhile, 86-year-old Douglas and 76-year-old Joan bond over a shared love of musicals, and social worker Becky 36, meets blockbuster movie safety officer Dave during her second visit

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson presents the dating show in which two singletons choose their favourite from a line-up of six potential dates who reveal themselves one body part at a time - in all their naked glory. First up is 43-year-old Cathy from Hertfordshire who, with her biological clock ticking, is wanting to settle down and have children. The trouble is she's open to dating men from their 20s to their 60s and finds it hard to choose. Will she find it easier to pick the right partner when they're all naked? Then, 24-year-old Londoner Adam takes centre stage. He hates online dating and has decided to bare all in order to find the perfect match

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. This time, Judith, a 57-year-old breast cancer survivor from Nuneaton, and Rigby, a 27-year-old hot tub installer from Chesterfield, take part

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The nation's favourite armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, offering passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of Britain's Got Talent, Attenborough's Passion Projects: A Blank on the Map, Alien, Sunday Brunch, Hillsborough, MasterChef and the News. First shown in May 2016

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: The animated comedy returns with Rick hosting a dinner for his ocean-dwelling nemesis, while Morty gets the wine. With the guest voice of Jim Gaffigan.

programme.title: Birdgirl
programme.desc: Birdgirl is named CEO of Sebben and Sebben and struggles to find a work/superhero balance. Animated comedy about superheroes working for a law firm, with the voice of Paget Brewster

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon pushes himself to the limit when he collaborates on two scientific projects - one with Leonard and Howard, plus another with Amy - on the same day. Bernadette nears the end of her maternity leave, and looks forward to returning to work

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard and Bernadette find it upsetting to leave baby Halley at the nursery when Bernadette returns to work at the end of her maternity leave. The gang's university colleague Bert the geologist introduces them to his attractive new girlfriend Rebecca

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Two daters returning to the restaurant include recruitment consultant Jack, 27, who is unwilling to change for anyone. Will he measure up for 21-year-old hairdresser Shauna? Meanwhile, 86-year-old Douglas and 76-year-old Joan bond over a shared love of musicals, and social worker Becky 36, meets blockbuster movie safety officer Dave during her second visit

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben, Danny and Tucker go in search of Emma's missing stuffed animal toy. Meanwhile Riley hosts a book club in a bid to make friends with the cool girls at work - but faces trouble when her mother and Bonnie invite themselves along. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau, with guest star Caroline Rhea

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley plans to break up with Fitch, but receives news that he is dead. She then meets Fitch's dad, Winston Douglas, who asks her to deliver the eulogy at the funeral, and he tells her Fitch planned to propose to her. Meanwhile, Ben realises he might be in love with Megan, but she thinks things are moving too quickly. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: JD and Turk struggle to adjust to their new roles as teachers with Sacred Heart now a med school campus. However, Dr Cox seems to have no trouble making his new students the targets of his acid-tongued rants, immediately setting his sights on fresh-faced Lucy Bennett. Comedy, starring Zach Braff, Donald Faison, John C McGinley and Kerry Bishe

programme.title: Scrubs
programme.desc: Dr Cox orders Lucy not to get emotionally attached to her patients, but when she ignores his advice, she is forced to learn her lesson the hard way. Meanwhile, Denise is attracted to an intern, but is hurt when she discovers he is not quite so enamoured with her. Medical comedy, starring Kerry Bishe and Eliza Coupe

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: When Rainbow makes a new friend at work who is having problems with her ex-husband, Dre realises that his wife has 'broken bird syndrome'. Andre Jr transforms his shared room with Jack into a frat house, so Ruby and Diane gang up against them

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Jannine and Shaun Cartwright build treasure chests for their young boys' toys with help from furniture-maker Anselm Fraser

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers solve a 20-year-old case and reveal the story of a pilot who died in the final days of the Second World War. Plus, semi-retired weather presenter Michael Fish discovers his family history and learns he is the heir to an unclaimed estate

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole tries to restore a monkey bike, and Simon O'Brien makes use of all his skills as he tries to give new life to an old racing bicycle

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien finds a barn containing a collection of replica Rolls-Royce cars, and Henry Cole tries his hand at driving a tractor as they continue their money-making tour

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to Brighton to secretly restore a 1984 Audi UR Quattro, which has been under a tarpaulin on a drive for 10 years. Owner Barry has dreamed of working on the vehicle, but health issues have left him sapped of energy and unable to fulfil his wish

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Two hoteliers prepare for their first big booking in France, it is crunch time for a British restaurant in Spain and a new boat business is stranded when the engine malfunctions

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Edenmore Guest House in Ardrossan, Ayrshire, where things start well for owner Hugh Begbie when his guests soon make themselves at home. However, a game of curling soon brings out their competitive sides, and a rusty pole causes a stir

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competitors are invited to Grassington Lodge in the market town of Grassington in the Yorkshire Dales, where owner Diane Lowe believes her establishment will impress with its 'little touches'. The guests enjoy their eggs Benedict breakfast and Diane receives some kind comments, but fellow proprietor Hugh takes some convincing that she is not a snob

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jeanette and Andrew Collette open the doors to Manor Farm in Oxfordshire, where the guests seem impressed on arrival - but the peaceful, rural feel doesn't appeal to Elle and Ian Betchley, and self-confessed 'dour' Scotsman Hugh Begbie just wants to be left alone without Jeanette's fussing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ian and Elle Betchley, owners of the George and Dragon Hotel in Princes Risborough, Buckinghamshire, welcome the contestants to their B&B, but some of them are not impressed to discover a stained teapot and dirty rooms. The duo organise an afternoon of ghost-hunting at the nearby Hellfire Caves, however there is a lack of paranormal activity. At breakfast, Diane and Sandra do not rate the eggs Benedict, leaving the hosts with mixed feedback at check-out time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners confront their rivals and discover what they have been paid, and emotions run high as the winner is announced. The host of Edenmore Guest House, Hugh Begbie, deals with criticism of the state of his exterior, Jeanette and Andrew from Manor Farm in Oxfordshire dejectedly analyse why their newfound friends would not stay again, and Elle and Ian Betchley from the George and Dragon in Buckinghamshire tackle Hugh on his hosting score

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: A man tries to build two homes at the same time, one for him and one for his mother - and to make things more difficult, both are situated on the top of a sand dune

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Cameras follow adolescent babirusa Tolo, who lives with his devoted mother Sula. They are very close, enjoying mud baths and pedicures together. But Sula has a hoof problem so they must be separated for the vet to intervene, and keepers are concerned for Tolo's wellbeing in her absence. Meanwhile, laid-back rock hyraxes Daissie and her partner Nungu are expecting their eighth litter. With the birth imminent, the couple's life of ease could be a thing of the past

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The plumbers race to install basic heating and water as Dick, Angel and the Strawbridge family decide it's time to move into their crumbling chateau. They'll be sharing their new home with bats, birds and thousands of flies, but Angel is in love with their new home and the clan begins the process of adjusting to French country life

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: GP Peter Berkin and his wife Chard, an alternative medicine practitioner, have decided to build a new home at the bottom of their garden, near Milton Keynes, but cannot agree on any part of the design - even the basic shape. Peter is a keen hobbyist who wants the house to feature a workshop where he can build a plane, and he's planning to spend twice the £200,000 budget Chard has in mind. Kevin McCloud follows their progress

programme.title: The Windsors: Inside the Royal Dynasty
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the royal family in the 20th and 21st centuries. The first episode focuses on the succession crisis following the death of George V, where Prince Edward was forced to choose between claiming the throne and marrying American divorcee Wallis Simpson

programme.title: Spying on the Royals
programme.desc: The conclusion of the two-part documentary about surveillance on Edward VIII and Wallis Simpson. In the wake of the abdication, the couple took a holiday in America, unaware they were being spied on by US intelligence agencies as well as British. The outbreak of the Second World War saw them move on to Lisbon, where their dealings with a known Nazi collaborator raised concerns for MI5

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Two families come to terms with life-changing events, including the relatives of eight-year-old Junior, who has an open leg fracture and suspected internal bleeding after being knocked off his bike by a car. His mum Bekki reveals how Junior learned to cope with extraordinary setbacks and how she worries about his future because of health problems of her own. Meanwhile, 46-year-old Anthony arrives in A&E after suffering chest pains during a day out at the races. As doctors attempt to get to the bottom of what's causing his dangerously high heart rate, his wife discusses how Anthony's awareness of his mortality was increased by losing his father

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Jon Richardson and Cariad Lloyd taking on guest captain Alan Carr and Kevin Bridges. Elis James and John Robins join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: The Windsors: Inside the Royal Dynasty
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the royal family in the 20th and 21st centuries. The first episode focuses on the succession crisis following the death of George V, where Prince Edward was forced to choose between claiming the throne and marrying American divorcee Wallis Simpson

programme.title: Spying on the Royals
programme.desc: The conclusion of the two-part documentary about surveillance on Edward VIII and Wallis Simpson. In the wake of the abdication, the couple took a holiday in America, unaware they were being spied on by US intelligence agencies as well as British. The outbreak of the Second World War saw them move on to Lisbon, where their dealings with a known Nazi collaborator raised concerns for MI5

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of favourite investigations, including Jimmy Doherty heading to southern Italy to find out where the flavour comes from in his Earl Grey tea. Kate Quilton wants to know if it is true that oysters can be eaten all year round, or whether they should be avoided in the summer months, while Matt Tebbutt is in Cornwall to learn what gives clotted cream its distinctive yellow colour

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Master sculptor and gilder Adam Williamson teaches Kirstie Allsop how to apply gold leaf to a second-hand picture frame to give it a luxury look and feel

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers try to track down the relatives of a Liverpudlian woman, and uncover the surprising connection between the site of a Welsh school and a former president of America. Plus, the details of unclaimed estates for which heirs still need to be found

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien arrive in York, where Henry struggles to squeeze into some motorcycle leathers and Simon gets into deep water when he tries to restore an aluminium boat

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a stately home in Somerset, where Simon is convinced he has uncovered a genuine Tudor relic

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend fix up a historic racing car, an Austin Healey Sprite, which has been kept in a garage since 1968. The challenge is to restore the car so that Sir Stirling Moss, who raced in the vehicle at Sebring in the USA in 1962, can get back behind the wheel

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Two British B&B owners battle with teething problems, an English-run boating company takes a large booking, and a home renovation project is jeopardised when a staircase collapses

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Langsmeade House in Thame, Oxfordshire, where Dutch-born owner Marianne Abben and her housekeeper Fran Birch hope to demonstrate that their B&B offers the best value for money. The pair's quirky hosting style proves a hit with some of their guests, before others reveal their competitive side during a Charleston dance lesson

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Stratford House Guesthouse in Stratford-upon-Avon on their second outing, where couple Natasha and Dai Pollock hope to impress their peers. But things do not run smoothly, as the guests are left unimpressed with the decor and discover nasty surprises in some of the drawers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third B&B of the week is pet-friendly Edmund House in Deal, Kent, where owner Michael Prime is joined by his mum Lorna. On arrival, Marianne Abben and Fran Birch love the seaside-inspired room, but entrepreneur Mark Genders is far from thrilled by the 'no frills' experience. Over dinner, tensions run high after self-confessed trouble-maker Marianne asks Mark to reveal which of the group he would and would not employ, and Lorna gets short shrift when she puts forward her application

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Mark Genders welcomes the contestants to the Five Bells Pub in Berkshire and hopes they will approve of his small but perfectly formed rooms. He organises an ale-tasting activity, although it fails to please all of his guests. Michael and Lorna admit they have seen a new side of Mark - however, will it affect their feedback?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners settle some scores and discover how much they have been paid, before the winner is revealed. Marianne and Fran from Langsmeade House begin by explaining that their worn rug is in fact a 200-year-old antique, Natasha and Dal from Stratford House confront Michael and Lorna about their strong language and cleaning techniques, while Mark Genders compares his experience at Edmund House to flying with a low budget airline

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Kate and Ian want to live in the remote Kenepuru Sound, an area that many workers refuse to go to, forcing the couple to build offsite and truck the home along a dangerous road

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the most compelling and memorable stories from the past five years, beginning with a series of extraordinary births caught on camera at Chester Zoo. Rhinoceros hornbill Liv and partner Manu are yet to produce any offspring, but when they are moved to a new nest, Liv quickly produces a fresh clutch of egg. Meanwhile, clumsy seahorse Seabiscuit only has a week to attract a female, so he has time to incubate the eggs and give birth in time for the next full moon

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel Adoree insists that Dick Strawbridge smash a hole through one of the castle's three-foot-thick interior walls to create a honeymoon suite, but he has far too much to do on his own and the schedule slips, badly

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Architect Patrick Bradley has come up with an unusual £100,000 house design built out of four 45ft shipping containers, welded together to form a giant cross and cantilevered over the top of a stream on the family farm in Co Derry. His mother is hoping the new home will help her son find a girlfriend, but the small budget and tight schedule soon pose problems. Kevin McCloud follows his progress

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a 17-year-old builder who has transformed a narrowboat into a floating holiday home, and a bride-to-be who has bought a disused burger van with the intention of turning it into a mobile bar for her wedding reception. George's Spanish tour continues with a visit to a snail-shaped house in the mountain valleys of Aragon

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: The idyllic village of Hartley Wintney is often voted one of the best places to live in Britain - but every rose has its thorn. Fiona and Hugh's ugly Hampshire home has not been touched in decades, and they hate absolutely everything except the garden. They have £180,000 budget to transform the entire house, so George enlists award-winning architect Greg Blee. However, while the owners buy into his modernist scheme the local planners do not. Can he salvage his design and come up with a scheme that will tick all the boxes?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Greg, 47, is airlifted to St George's with critical injuries to his arm and leg after being knocked off his motorbike on the way into work. As his wife Lucie rushes to his bedside, she's shocked by what she finds. Cameras also follow, Cara, 11, who has fallen from the top of a human pyramid doing gymnastics and has a broken arm that doctors must try to realign

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with Kathy Burke and Roisin Conaty taking on Johnny Vegas and Bob Mortimer. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a 17-year-old builder who has transformed a narrowboat into a floating holiday home, and a bride-to-be who has bought a disused burger van with the intention of turning it into a mobile bar for her wedding reception. George's Spanish tour continues with a visit to a snail-shaped house in the mountain valleys of Aragon

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Greg, 47, is airlifted to St George's with critical injuries to his arm and leg after being knocked off his motorbike on the way into work. As his wife Lucie rushes to his bedside, she's shocked by what she finds. Cameras also follow, Cara, 11, who has fallen from the top of a human pyramid doing gymnastics and has a broken arm that doctors must try to realign

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads to Paris to find out why baguettes go stale so much faster than sliced loaves of bread. Matt Tebbutt is in Italy to investigate why buffalo mozzarella is much more expensive than that from cows and Briony May Williams discovers the surprising secret to the making of mushy peas

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp demonstrates how forgotten furniture from a landfill site can be transformed, as she teams up with furniture-maker Simon Bell to revive a dining-room chair using clever painting tricks

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers look into the case of a former member of the Royal College of Surgeons, and race to discover the rightful owners of his inheritance. The team also investigates the case of a woman whose father was a famous pigeon fancier. Plus, details of some unclaimed estates requiring heirs

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien restore eight items, including a pine blanket chest, a pair of battlefield telephones and some gothic church windows

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Wales, where they pick up and restore a dentist's table and a barber's chair, and make a coffee table out of parquet flooring

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to Northumberland to restore a real 'barn find'; a 1934 Morgan F4 three-wheeler, which has been stored in a shed for more than 50 years. The vehicle was one of the most successful lightweight cars from the early days of motoring

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Two British ice-cream lovers move to France and start churning it out. The owners of a gite complex in the Dordogne have to troubleshoot, and two English artists resort to selling their own home as costs of their B&B build spiral out of control

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Trecarne House in Liskeard, Cornwall, where owners Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan hope to demonstrate that their B&B offers the best value for money. The pair win the group's admiration by sharing their life story, but one guest stirs up intrigue by holding his cards close to his chest

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners spend their second night of the competition at the Magna Carta at Windsor in Berkshire, a five-star floating bed and breakfast run by Captain Dominic Read. The guests are pleasantly surprised upon arrival, but not all are impressed by the standard of the accommodation - and a cracked teapot at the breakfast table leads to trouble for the proprietor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third B&B of the week is Ash Cottage near Leeds Castle in Kent, where the unusual decor is an eye-opener for the guests but is not everyone's cup of tea. Trish and Tom get heated over the instant coffee before owners Rayne and Chris Deal take their fellow contestants to Chatham Dockyard to try their hands at rope-making

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final guest house of the week is A Room with a View in Brighton, where owner Stephen Bull and housekeeper Joanna Harris hope their nine-room establishment will impress the contestants. Things get off to a good start when Rayne and Chris Deal are impressed by the cleanliness of the property, but things take a turn for the worse when breakfast gets the thumbs down

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together to confront their critics face-to-face. Hosts of Trecarne House in Cornwall Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan raise issues with Magna Carta's Dominic Read on his mark for their hosting, while he has a bone to pick with them over criticism of his five-star floating B&B. Stephen and Joanna of A Room with a View in Brighton receive complaints about their breakfast, and tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: In Auckland, Shane and Tina Nicholls design a unique family home using Japanese burnt timber and Scandinavian brick, with a special memorial garden, views over the gulf, a wine cellar and a pool

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: More of the most memorable moments from the past five years, including one of the zoo's most enduring love stories at Penguin Island. At 22, Rudd is the oldest penguin on the island and has been with his partner Spike for more than 15 years. Meanwhile, Silvery gibbon Alvin is keen to get close to his partner Tilu again, but ever since they had a baby together, young Eko has proven a bit of a passion killer

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree only have a few weeks to prepare for their wedding, but the chateau is nowhere near ready to receive the 200 guests they have invited and finding reliable, affordable workers is proving difficult. Dick oversees the damming of the moat and the installation of a new, eco-friendly sewage system, while Angel turns her attention to giving the rooms a distinctive decor

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Tracy and Steve Fox want to build an 'urban shed' featuring two studios, three bedrooms and a double-height space with a walkway, made out of industrial-style materials including corrugated cement fibreboard and 20ft panels of polycarbonate sheeting. Having discovered an old milk yard tucked away behind some flats and shops in south-east London, their long-held desire to build a highly unconventional family home and workspace begins. But their choice of unorthodox materials proves challenging and costly. Kevin McCloud follows their progress

programme.title: Coroner
programme.desc: The final episode of the Canadian drama following Dr Jenny Cooper, a recently widowed Toronto coroner, who investigates suspicious, unnatural or sudden deaths in the city. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to improve the ambulance, with the presenters all going their own separate ways to enhance one area they think could do with an overhaul. programme.desc: How the Nazis searched for religious items. However, after their defeat, an American detachment known as ‘The Monuments Men' found and repatriated a large proportion of the loot

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of Hudson Yards on Manhattan Island, New York, a construction project built over the tracks and railway sidings of Penn Station - literally in thin air

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: An insight into the construction of the cutting-edge Ford class aircraft carriers, the largest warships ever built, which are almost as long as the Empire State Building is tall

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the Conwy Valley in north Wales, stopping in Betws-y-Coed to find out about Britain's first artists' colony. He also visits the Victorian slate capital Blaenau Ffestiniog and takes a steam train to Porthmadog harbour

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Ledbury to Holyhead, taking the train to the summit of Wales' highest peak, Mount Snowdon. He then examines the revival of Anglesey's sea salt industry, before discovering how the railways transformed the port of Holyhead

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special episode from the Houses of Parliament celebrating the 100th anniversary of women winning the right to vote, examining artefacts connected to remarkable women throughout British history, including leading suffragist Millicent Fawcett, photographer Christina Broom, secret agent Violette Szabo, gymnast Florence Atwood and polar explorers Ann Daniels and Caroline Hamilton

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Documentary exploring unusual rail networks from around the world, beginning with a look at trains in cities, including an inverted monorail in Wuppertal, Germany. With contributions from Susana Esteves da Fonseca

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: High-definition cameras and aerial photography techniques are used to explore the archipelago, which is rich in wildlife and made famous by Charles Darwin's On the Origin of the Species. This programme focuses on the geological history of the islands through the evidence of volcanic eruptions and examines what makes it unique. Tilda Swinton narrates

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the world of rodents, whose constantly growing incisor teeth are used to chisel through any obstacle and range from squirrels and the English harvest mouse to the large South American capybara. He looks at examples of their dental prowess, showing how agoutis in Panama break open rock-hard nutshells and the skills employed by beavers in Wyoming as they fell massive trees to build dams rivalling feats of human engineering

programme.title: The Day They Dropped the Bomb
programme.desc: Documentary marking the 70th anniversary of the day the world's first atomic bomb was detonated above the Japanese city of Hiroshima on August 6, 1945. Featuring a minute-by-minute account of what happened on that tragic day, through archive footage and interviews with eyewitnesses who rarely speak about their experience of the bombing. Also interviewed is navigator Theodore Van Kirk, who was the last surviving member of the crew who dropped the bomb, before his death in 2014

programme.title: Nazi Victory: The Post-War Plan
programme.desc: The documentary concludes with an insight into Hitler's plans to modernise the German transport system, including super trains and futuristic airports

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special episode from the Houses of Parliament celebrating the 100th anniversary of women winning the right to vote, examining artefacts connected to remarkable women throughout British history, including leading suffragist Millicent Fawcett, photographer Christina Broom, secret agent Violette Szabo, gymnast Florence Atwood and polar explorers Ann Daniels and Caroline Hamilton

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey, travelling from Newcastle upon Tyne to Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, along some of the earliest railways in England. He visits the first locomotive factory in the world, opened by George Stephenson, searches for the lost pit village of Marsden in South Shields, and is entertained by a comic troupe of rapping sword-dancers in Chester-le-Street, Co Durham

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Newcastle to Melton Mowbray, stopping at historic Durham Cathedral. He also explores one of the first locomotives in Darlington, and takes a gothic Dracula-inspired tour of Whitby. Finally, he boards a steam train to travel across the North Yorkshire Moors

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey around north-west England by celebrating Victorian trade with the Preston Guild, and then heading to Rochdale to learn about a pioneering movement to improve the lives of working families. The presenter also follows in the tracks of many 19th-century industrial employees who made day trips to Hebden Bridge to walk in the Calder Valley

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses into West Yorkshire to begin the next leg of his journey, visiting the Worth Valley and the home of the three Bronte sisters, who were early investors in the railway industry. He then heads to Oakworth to find out how its station and tracks were used in one of the most popular films ever made, and in Bradford discovers the ways in which 19th-century workers saved to buy a home. The presenter finishes in Halifax, where he learns how the railways contributed to the town's success

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare Honda S800 sports car from 1967 takes Derek's fancy, and a couple of very different Ford Capris with long histories go head-to-head at the auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewsons sell a 1958 Bedford D122 and a 1969 Vanden Plas Princess. They also meet a collector who may be willing to finally part with his 220-car collection

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber takes up boxing and enters the prison championships. He is hot favourite to win until Fletcher is given the task of fixing the contest and nobbling his cell-mate. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale and Peter Vaughan

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next leg of his journey, travelling from Newcastle upon Tyne to Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, along some of the earliest railways in England. He visits the first locomotive factory in the world, opened by George Stephenson, searches for the lost pit village of Marsden in South Shields, and is entertained by a comic troupe of rapping sword-dancers in Chester-le-Street, Co Durham

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Newcastle to Melton Mowbray, stopping at historic Durham Cathedral. He also explores one of the first locomotives in Darlington, and takes a gothic Dracula-inspired tour of Whitby. Finally, he boards a steam train to travel across the North Yorkshire Moors

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Etaples Military Cemetery in France, as the team discovers the personal stories behind treasured objects associated with the First World War. Among the unique items brought in are two poppies that were picked from Ypres in 1915, a tray that was made by a solider blinded by mustard gas, and a document that was part of the political process to bring the war to an end. Plus, the stories of an Indian army surgeon and a volunteer nurse and ambulance driver who became the first woman to earn the Military Medal for bravery

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: The wilderness lines that have pushed the frontiers of human endeavour into previously unknown territories, solving a set of complex problems along the way

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: Examining the lesser-known parts of the islands and the species that have successfully evolved to suit the environment. The programme discovers how tortoise shells have changed shape, flowers have turned yellow to attract bees, and finches have become warblers. Plus, how flightless cormorants are still hunting underwater. Narrated by Tilda Swinton

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the hunting tactics of the world's carnivores. Arctic foxes in the frozen north must collect food during warmer times to survive the winter, while the big cats of the southern hemisphere rely on stealth and surprise to catch each meal. The presenter also reveals how leopards and tigers are solitary hunters, coming together only to mate, whereas lions stay in family groups so they can tackle larger prey and better protect their young

programme.title: The Day When
programme.desc: Pivotal moments in history recreated using archive materials and dramatic reconstructions, beginning with Churchill persuading the British government to go to war

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers

programme.desc: Competitive golf-loving couple Lynn and Mike from Southampton have been holidaying in Spain for 40 years and even lived there previously. They love it so much they now wish to now buy a holiday home there, so Danni Menzies lines up five options for their £60,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gary Fitzpatrick and Shahine Korimbocus want a holiday home in the tranquil Limousin area of France where they can enjoy an outdoor lifestyle with their 20-month-old twins Charlie and Catherine, and Gary's son Dominic. Brought up with French culture in Mauritius, Shahine wants the children to share her enjoyment of it, and to pick up the language. With a budget of £88,000, Scarlette Douglas sets out to help the family find their perfect house

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps good friends Mike Dawson and Louise Clark search for a holiday apartment in the resort of Sitges, near Barcelona, on a budget of £170,000. Louise is hoping for somewhere to escape with her two-year-old daughter, while Mike is looking to make the first step in a potentially permanent move

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: NHS manager Helen Mather holds the first party in Leicester, hoping her plans for a simple menu will allow her more time to entertain her guests. However, the accident-prone host has a mishap involving raw pig's blood, and tries to turn the evening around with some 'urban ballet' after one diner sours the mood with a display of less-than-polite table manners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Terry Robinson hosts the second dinner party in Leicester with a 'Dress as Your Hero' theme, and the Elton John fan also decides to treat his guests to a song. He is confident his menu will score him top marks, but while Richard is blown away, Ryan remains unimpressed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty spa manager Porchia Lee hosts the third dinner party in Leicester, serving up a French-themed menu of chicken liver pate, pan-fried chicken with tarragon sauce and creme brulee. Unfortunately for her the pudding is so bad her guests are reduced to tears, and double glazing salesman Richard Deeping insults her appearance

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: IT enthusiast Ryan Bruce hosts the fourth dinner party in Leicester, and tries to impress his guests with a Spanish-inspired menu cooked in front of them. Entertainment is provided by Ryan's sister, West End actress Charlie Bruce, who puts the contestants through their paces in a salsa lesson

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Double-glazing salesman Richard Deeping hosts the final dinner party in Leicester, and hopes to make up for his boisterous behaviour by impressing the guests with his culinary prowess. In a bid to land higher scores, he puts on his own brand of entertainment, only to reveal a little too much of himself in the process, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels across the United States in search of restaurants in dire need of renovation - and after analysing their situation, he has just 24 hours to bring them back from the brink of disaster. His first stop is the New York community of Congers, where he hopes to save family-run Italian restaurant Bella Gianna's. Gordon and his team work round the clock to clean the premises, remodel the interior and revamp the menu, while also teaching the staff to prepare new dishes and work together as an efficient unit. What will the customers think of the new-look restaurant?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie has big plans to give her ground-floor apartment in her chateau's east wing a Renaissance revival, but support from Gerry and mother Isabelle turns out to be more of a hindrance than help. Sarah and George secure their first wedding booking and prepare to woo the couple as they visit the chateau for a planning meeting. Debbie and Nigel start renovating their chateau's salon, but need to down tools quickly as they get an unexpected last-minute booking

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Karen and Julian run a B&B in Cornwall and although they've never been to Naxos, they've decided it's the perfect place for a holiday home. Scarlette Douglas sets out to help them find the perfect pad on a budget of £215,000

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Part one of two. Veteran actors and married couple Prunella Scales and Timothy West pass the tiller to Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth, who are celebrating 40 years of friendship by making new memories together and learning new skills. Sheila and Gyles learn the ropes with Tim, and set out by themselves along the upper reaches of the River Thames. It's the same river that roars past their homes in London, but just 50 miles upstream it's an Arcadian paradise of unspoiled nature, royal retreats and river regattas

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: The head gardener at Cornwall's Lost Gardens of Heligan sows the seeds of a wildflower revolution from a colourful crop of meadow flowers to help the rest of the county bloom. A trawler skipper live-streams his latest catch via Facebook to his customers, before selling direct to them off his boat when he returns to harbour. An engineer gets a replica of iconic locomotive the Puffing Devil back up to full steam, and a Devon wildlife ranger takes care of wild beavers

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Several of Britain's best-loved personalities turn their hand to being the country's most opinionated viewers. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the week's biggest news stories, and from hard-hitting documentary series to gritty drama, they offer sharp, insightful, funny and sometimes emotional critiques of popular and topical TV shows

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A nurse shares her story of finding a career she loves, and doctors suspect a 93-year-old man with a low heart rate, dizziness and confusion may need a new pacemaker fitting. A construction worker whose finger has been crushed discusses the importance of male role models in his life, and a haemophiliac reflects on living with his condition

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The realities of policing home drinking, and what happens when parties go very badly wrong, seen through the eyes of South Yorkshire Police's frontline officers. PCs Alice Marshall and Matt Spaven investigate a house party that's a concern for neighbours, while PCs Mohammed Alrobaidi and Bernice Gott find themselves at risk at a rowdy house party in a quiet street. In Rotherham, PCs Pete Ellard and Tabitha Rumney have their hands full with a drunk and disorderly couple who are violently resisting arrest

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics rush to a man who has overdosed on heroin in a public toilet in Bromsgrove, and a 73-year-old man with slurred speech is tested to determine if he has had a stroke

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Tom Allen and Liam Charles present as the pastry chefs create miniature cone-shaped and carrot-shaped desserts - 24 of each. For the second challenge they must serve up a grand occasion showpiece - a Religieuse a l'Ancienne - to serve 24 people, with two different flavours of eclairs and inspired by the theme of time travel. Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden judge their efforts

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Nathan and Amye as they build a giant cathedral-like property modelled on Dutch barn style houses, but with a contemporary twist. It is a hugely ambitious plan, and with Nathan project managing and setting themselves just a year to finish the build, the pressure to pull it off is huge

programme.title: Coast vs Country
programme.desc: Kerr Drummond shows Tom and Colette the very best of what their £1,200,000 budget can get them on the Dorset coast, while Kirsty Duffy takes them to view rural properties

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The group members take part in a traditional cooking class in Lagos, southern Portugal, followed by a trip to see dolphins

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week the contestants head to The Black Swan Inn in Pickering, Yorkshire, where husband and wife Phil and Jill Hall hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Glasgow pair Jean and Ron hope their 'taste of Scotland' menu will impress their guests. Day two sees Colin and Craig try to claw back the limelight and jolly up the mood

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tamsin is looking for her dream holiday home on the Greek island of Crete. She and her best friend Emma, who she has known since meeting at nursery school, have enjoyed many a holiday on the island, and now Tamsin is ready to have her own bolthole there. Laura Hamilton works within her budget of £100,000 to find a suitable property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a second home abroad

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Elsa and Brian Surridge were on the verge of buying a Spanish property when the 2008 financial crisis scared them off. A decade later they're back and Brian has promised Elsa they won't leave without bagging their dream retirement property on the Costa Blanca. Jasmine Harman shows them five very different options for their cash budget of £75,000.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Natasha Conti hosts the first dinner party in Salisbury, Wiltshire, and puts together an evening on the theme of a summer fete, complete with prizes for her fellow competitors. However, as the guests get to know each other, Chris Hiller finds himself in an awkward situation

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Farm manager Sophie McConnell hopes that her pig-racing entertainment will impress the guests on her night as host in Salisbury, Wiltshire. She also plans to serve her home-raised cow Levi for the main course

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Anthony Brown-Hovelt hosts the third dinner party in Salisbury, Wiltshire, and his menu includes Uzbekistani food and porridge ice-cream. The former army major dresses as an American sailor, but Natasha Conti does not find the outfit as appealing as she had hoped

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Housing support officer Chris Hiller plans an eclectic but affordable menu for his night in Salisbury, Wiltshire, including chicken a l'orange and peppers stuffed with baked beans. However, with the sole focus being on keeping costs low, his efforts might not go down well with the other guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Care worker Jane Arnold hosts the final dinner party in Salisbury, Wiltshire, hoping to impress her guests with food from her local area. However, it is her bedroom revelations that catch the diners' attentions, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is given just 24 hours to improve troubled American restaurants, continuing with in New Orleans with the Old Coffee Pot, a century-old bistro that used to be one of the best breakfast spots in the area. Gordon and his team brighten up the kitchen and dining area and introduce new dishes. But what will the staff think of the changes?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Debbie and Nigel start landscaping their five-acre grounds and risk a flat-pack fall out. Also requiring urgent attention is the tired roof terrace at the rear of the chateau which currently isn't watertight. Sarah and George run into trouble with the chateau's electrics as they host their first-ever wedding. Dick gets to work re-levelling the chateau's long driveway and Stephanie turns her attention to a new, modern apartment in the chateau's old stables

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps George Robertson from Bedfordshire search for a second home in Majorca's Port de Pollensa, a place he has visited over 30 times

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: The head gardener at Cornwall's Lost Gardens of Heligan sows the seeds of a wildflower revolution from a colourful crop of meadow flowers to help the rest of the county bloom. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A nurse shares her story of finding a career she loves, and doctors suspect a 93-year-old man with a low heart rate, dizziness and confusion may need a new pacemaker fitting. A construction worker whose finger has been crushed discusses the importance of male role models in his life, and a haemophiliac reflects on living with his condition

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Denise van Outen and Eddie Boxshall, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, Iain Stirling and Laura Whitmore, Sunetra Sarker and Georgia Taylor, Lorraine Kelly and Rosie Smith, Melanie C and her brother Paul, Clare Balding and Alice Arnold, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, and Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford offer sharp, insightful, funny and sometimes emotional critiques of popular and topical TV shows

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Nathan and Amye as they build a giant cathedral-like property modelled on Dutch barn style houses, but with a contemporary twist. It is a hugely ambitious plan, and with Nathan project managing and setting themselves just a year to finish the build, the pressure to pull it off is huge

programme.title: Dogging Tales
programme.desc: True Stories documentary by photographer Leo Maguire, providing an insight into why men and women engage in or watch sexual activity in front of strangers in public areas after dark. Accompanying his participants to lay-bys, woods and picnic spots around the UK, they discuss their attraction to dogging and reveal how they were introduced to it

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A 23-year-old virgin from Kent picks a partner from six nude candidates - but this proves a nerve-racking experience, since he has never seen a naked woman in his life. Meanwhile, a 27-year-old beauty therapist from London is determined to have a baby before she turns 30, and hopes that one of the men lurking behind the screens will be the perfect father

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A final romp through the Naked Attraction archive, remembering the times that the pickers got it spot on and chose their perfect date, leading to romance and happy endings. The success stories include Gemma and James, who found love on the show and whose relationship is still going strong

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The tourists hit the Algarve beach resort of Portimao, where their day begins with a pottery class - only for disaster to strike later

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Delamere Farm near Peterborough, where proud owner Agi Chapman is sent into a spin after hearing some critical comments over breakfast

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Sussex, childhood sweethearts Jemma and James kick off with a menu based on their unlikely romance, finding a cold soup starter divides opinions. On day two, soon-to-be-retired dinner party veterans Phil and Sue cook up a menu of home comfort food, but disaster strikes in the dessert. On the final day, critical Steve and Aaron prepare a pescatarian feast with a fish-shaped salmon Wellington. Once again James and Jemma have to defend their relationship due to their less than romantic behaviour

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tracey and Dean met in their early twenties and have devoted their lives to raising their family, including their own three children and two foster children. Now that they are finally in a position to buy a holiday home in the Mar Menor in Spain, Jasmine Harman is on hand to show them five properties for their £70,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: After becoming foster parents to three young children, Jo Danson and her husband Bill decided a holiday home would be the perfect way for everyone to spend quality time together. Working within a budget of £65,000, Jasmine Harman shows Jo five suitable properties in the Orihuela Costa region of Spain

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin searches Lot-et-Garonne in south-west of France for Karen Wright and Philip Johnston, who dream of retiring to the country. They have £100,000, but that isn't just for them. The property needs to be large enough for their five dogs - and to accommodate even more as they have a dream of fostering rescue pooches

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Virginia Houslin opens the contest in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, and her hopes for some flirtatious fun are boosted by the arrival of her first guest, wedding singer Richard Wood. However the cheeky host faces a battle to win over the taste buds of her other diners as she tries to stake her claim for the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business development manager Iyesha Atkins hosts the second dinner party in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, hoping her sophisticated event will win her the £1,000 prize. However, the evening does not go to plan when Virginia finds fault with Iyesha's main course, and the two women fall out following a discussion about religion

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wedding singer and confident cook Richard Wood hosts the third dinner party in Huddersfield. With steak on the menu, he opts not to listen to his guests' preferences and instead cooks and serves them his own way. However, with the starter a disaster and the rift between mum-of-three Virginia and ladette Iyesha deepening, Richard believes the only solution to save his night is for him to sing

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tommy Mack hosts the fourth dinner-party challenge from Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, and tries to woo his guests with a Hollywood silent movie-themed evening, which includes a selection of mystery dishes and his very own style of rapping

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Kim Connor hosts the final party in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, hoping her American diner-themed menu will delight her guests. Sweating over a hot stove is not what the glamorous hairdresser is used to, and she relies on helpful advice from her mother. The food fails to appeal to everyone's tastes, but Kim's after-dinner entertainment seems to revive her flagging chances of success

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to the upmarket quarter of Hollywood to help the owner of failing health-food restaurant Sante La Brea. As well as struggling to control his disrespectful staff, the proprietor receives a surprise visit from the police at the height of dinner service

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Phillip aims to transform three derelict rooms at Chateau de la Fare into the perfect bridal suite for his daughter Penny's wedding in three months' time

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Colin and Elspeth from Edinburgh search for a holiday home in Menorca, which they can enjoy well into their retirement with their family

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Darrell from south-west London are looking for a holiday home on the Balearic Island of Menorca to enjoy with their two young children and Darrell's grown-up son. They run their own busy human resources company together so cannot wait to find a bolthole to relax in and have some quality time as a family. With a budget of £300,000 Danni Menzies is on hand to show them five properties around the south of the island

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at 20 years of trying to find homes for people with very specific criteria and must-haves. Ghika and Ronnie's search area is roughly two square miles, and they have been looking for the perfect house for a decade. Surfing is Jamie's passion, so he and wife Sam are looking for somewhere a stone's throw from the beach. Musicians Me'sha and Darren have viewed 70 properties but cannot seem to let go of anything on their wish list

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The realities of policing home drinking, and what happens when parties go very badly wrong, seen through the eyes of South Yorkshire Police's frontline officers. PCs Alice Marshall and Matt Spaven investigate a house party that's a concern for neighbours, while PCs Mohammed Alrobaidi and Bernice Gott find themselves at risk at a rowdy house party in a quiet street. In Rotherham, PCs Pete Ellard and Tabitha Rumney have their hands full with a drunk and disorderly couple who are violently resisting arrest

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Emilia Fox and David Wilson investigate the 1967 murder of 19-year-old RAF servicewoman Rita Ellis, whose body was found by a dog walker near her base, having been killed in a brutal attack. Despite a number of leads the police have never managed to catch Rita's murderer and with a case that's 53 years old, Emilia and David have a challenge tracking down witnesses, other potential victims and a suspect

programme.title: Taken (2008)
programme.desc: An American teenager travelling through Europe is abducted by a gang of sex traffickers. However, what the kidnappers do not realise is that the girl's father is a former CIA agent, who employs all his old skills as he searches for the criminals and tries to rescue his daughter. Thriller, starring Liam Neeson, Famke Janssen and Maggie Grace

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A final romp through the Naked Attraction archive, remembering the times that the pickers got it spot on and chose their perfect date, leading to romance and happy endings. The success stories include Gemma and James, who found love on the show and whose relationship is still going strong

programme.title: Before We Die
programme.desc: Hannah and Billy know how the Mimicas plan to smuggle the drugs into the UK. But with Christian out of action, they have no clue when or where the consignment will be delivered. Meanwhile, when another drug dealer confesses to Sean's murder, it looks like the criminal family will get away with it - until Bianca makes a discovery that turns everything on its head

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: West Midlander Vince rushes to get his new bar ready, having mistakenly thought he was paying for a working bar when it turns out he had actually just bought the lease on the building - leaving him to do the rest himself. Meanwhile, former dancer Bambi plans a racy show to raise cash for a local dog rescue shelter, and Andy tours the coast buying and selling old furniture

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Fiddes family from Royal Wootton Bassett rank in the top 10 per cent of Britain's wealthy, and live in a six-bedroom home paid for by husband Matt's chain of martial arts centres. For the four-strong Leamon family, who are among the nation's poorest 10 per cent, the swap means leaving their four-bedroom terrace on a Southampton council estate, and enjoying a weekly budget of £1,500 instead of their usual £170

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Documenting the exploits of waste enforcers as they battle to keep Britain tidy. Featuring a tsunami of effluent in Hampshire, sewer safety and a wet wipe wild goose chase

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A scooter is driven the wrong way down a dual carriageway into oncoming traffic, but despite it having its tyres stung and being followed by a police helicopter, it proves incredibly difficult to apprehend the rider. A chase nearly ends in serious injury for an officer when he is almost dragged along by a car after it is cornered in a supermarket car park

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers stop a driver in relation to an alleged drunken assault and the breathalyser indicates he is more than three times over the limit and two knives are found in his car. Scott is patrolling Peak District villages and responds to a call in from a member of the public about two men acting suspiciously, soon coming across a white van towing an expensive trailer

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: An officer is nearly run over by a desperate driver, and another's patience is tested by a drunken motorist. Plus, a law enforcer goes in search of a suspicious character, and a group of youths tries to deceive the interception unit

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A helicopter is used in the pursuit of an armed robber, ending in a serious accident. A sniffer dog makes a discovery, a fraudster is taken into custody and a gang of lorry thieves is pursued in a major manhunt. Plus, new recruits at South Yorkshire's Road Crime Unit are put to the test by a pair of suspected burglars

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team closes in on a suspected petrol thief, stops a motorist with a stash of suspicious goods in his boot, and tracks a motorist who heads to the fair after ditching his car. The police also have an appointment with a drink-driver who crashed into a doctor's surgery, and the owner of an uninsured car tests the officers' patience to the limit

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall tries to help the owners of Bella the collie in Sheffield, whose aggression has become so extreme that people can no longer visit the house and the family cannot take the pet out in public. Graeme also meets Toby, a stroppy basset hound that enjoys sitting on the kitchen counter, and Dave, the French bulldog with a penchant for eating its own droppings

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Levi, Harlow and Yashvi commiserate over their relationship woes. Yashvi initiates a serious discussion with Ned, hoping to resolve their issues

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Though Nikau and Bella continue to chafe against Sienna's manipulative ways, the money is too good to turn down. Dean learns Tane and Ziggy have moved in together

programme.title: 10 Years Younger in 10 Days
programme.desc: Cherry Healey and the team help two woman desperate for a fresh start and dramatic transformations. Widow Jackie was left disabled after a skiing accident, while single mum Charlotte's life and looks have taken a back seat to caring for her four kids and managing their health conditions

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: From endless food and drink to hotels with kids' clubs and water parks, going all-inclusive is supposed to be all about hassle-free fun in the sun. But for some, this option proves anything but relaxing. Here cameras follow the unlucky holidaymakers whose breaks went horribly wrong. They include nurse Julia Vaughan, who spent more than £5,500 on a relaxing break in Crete for herself and her family, only to be confronted by a giant construction site next to their hotel

programme.title: The Clues That Caught the Killer
programme.desc: Documentary examining the vital breakthroughs that helped police catch the killers of 60-year-old Sadie Hartley, who was immobilised with a stun gun and stabbed to death in her own home in January 2016. Detectives quickly identified two suspects, but only patient and thorough police work could uncover the evidence to secure a conviction

programme.title: My Lover My Killer
programme.desc: The case of a disabled man who formed a close relationship with his carer and her son, a situation they exploited to ultimately murderous ends

programme.title: The Hunger Games (2012)
programme.desc: A totalitarian future holds an annual nationally televised event in which two children from each of its regions fight to the death until only one remains. A teenage girl takes her younger sister's place in the games and must pit her wits against the deadly combat skills of her rivals. Sci-fi adventure based on Suzanne Collins' novel, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Josh Hutcherson, Woody Harrelson and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: The Gypsies Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary about gypsies fighting to keep their traditions alive and the locals trying to ban them. Traveller Tony dreams of a new life for his family so he has set up a sunbed business with millionaire non-traveller Colin. However, the neighbours have got a court order to prevent him setting up the company. Margaret, a single mother of seven, just needs a roof over her head after being evicted, while Simey Connors prepares a grand funeral for his brother Mikey, a legend in the traveller community

programme.desc: At Holiday Resort Unity in Somerset, there's a surprise in store for soft play worker Lauren as Covid-19 restrictions are lifted and venues are reopened - and entertainment chief Chunkie Russell is finally allowed to welcome the guests into the main entertainment venue RJs. programme.title: Dance Moms
programme.desc: With no sign of Abby, the Irreplaceables and the ALDC both find new choreographers, including famed dancer Cheryl Burke

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Sheffield-born market trader Jacqui, who needs to make as much as she can at the tourist night markets on the Costa del Sol prom. There's also a look at cockney former publican Victor, who has spent the past 18 years living in his motor home. But with only his pension to live on, and his movable mansion in need of a refurb, will he have to sell up?

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Neil and Louise Brimicombe swap their three-bedroom house on a Watford estate to spend a week in the six-bedroom Chelmsford home of restaurateurs Steve and Jo Haslam - and vice versa - to see how the other half lives. The Brimicombes and their children will see their weekly budget increase from £140 to a massive £3,000, and will also enjoy nightly takeaways, three cars, regular pampering sessions and expensive shopping trips. Meanwhile, Jo is away on business so Steve will be left as a single parent - how will he cope given he has never even worked a washing machine?

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Special vigilante waste warriors Martin and Glenn opt for a stake out in their pursuit of a phantom fly-tipper terrorising their local neighbourhood

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: PC Andy Swift is patrolling on the M1 when a car he has flagged down takes off, prompting a chase at speeds up to 120mph, while PC Jason Potts is called to a pub where a distressed woman is convinced her boyfriend has fallen into the freezing waters of a canal

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Matt leads a team pursuing a seven-tonne truck carrying stolen goods. Dave and Rich help to box in a lorry with fake plates on the M1. Craig must balance closing the roads with solving the mystery of a pair of escaping passengers

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A driver mounts pavements, bumps into cars and collides with an ambulance as he tries to escape officers during a chase through the streets of Ripley in Derbyshire. An out-of-control car crashes into an interceptor's vehicle, and a helicopter is called in when a driver tries to lose his pursuers by taking the chase off-road

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A drink-driver is involved in a crash, a van full of criminals loses control in a high-speed pursuit, and the police helicopter is involved in a lengthy manhunt. Officers also deal with trouble on the streets of Derbyshire, and pursue a motorist who thinks he can outrun the law

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A drink-driver damages one of the team's favourite cars, and Kevin hunts down a horsebox full of illegal plants. Elsewhere, officers are led on a chase across the countryside, Nick and Dee offer an insight into Taser tactics, and a very important visitor arrives in Chesterfield

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Trainer Graeme Hall tries to solve more dog behavioural problems, visiting a family in East Sussex struggling with rescue puppy Diego, a Rottweiler-Rhodesian ridgeback cross. In Cambridgeshire, cocker spaniel Doug's relentless thievery is destroying any chance of a peaceful retirement for owners Julie and Mark, while Graeme's third stop is in Derby, where he meets two miniature schnauzers known locally as the Krays

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Chloe suspects that Levi and Harlow hooked up and shares the theory with Sheila and Mackenzie. Hendrix and Karl bond over money-saving driving tips

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Justin loses control when he realises he is short on medication for his trip. Ziggy worries about Dean, who has gone missing

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall helps identical twins who have been inseparable all their lives, but are now struggling because one sister's labrador cross is always bullying the other's rescue dog. Other clients include a French bulldog who hates TV and is always smashing holes in the set, and a sprocker who is constantly destroying things around the house

programme.title: Happy Campers: The Caravan Park
programme.desc: Holiday Resort Unit marketing guru Jonathan attempts to give old-school entertainment chief Chunkie Russell a new online look, while a Midlands couple are on a quest to buy their own static caravan. At Tencreek holiday park in Looe, yellow coat Andi unveils his alter ego Miss Champagne, and a new food-ordering app goes haywire, leaving catering manager Philomena all at sea

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Richard and Brennan come to the aid of a single father in Keighley, who has two sons, one dog, two cats and a pair of parrots to take care of - leaving little time for housework

programme.title: The Gypsies Next Door
programme.desc: In the Irish town of Thurles, the council want Barry to move into new housing purpose-built for travellers. But they will not allow him to take his 30 horses. Teenager Scarlett, who lives with her traveller family in Luton, dreams of being a successful model - and non-traveller model Danielle Mason is going to help show her the way

programme.title: Carrie (2013)
programme.desc: A repressed teenage girl is tormented at school by bullies and at home by her fanatically religious mother. She begins to develop psychic powers and when her prom night results in one final humiliation, she snaps and uses her abilities to wreak a terrible revenge on everyone who has made her life a misery. Horror remake, starring Chloe Grace Moretz and Julianne Moore
